abstract: This article examines medical image segmentation techniques applied to human internal organ computed tomography (CT) scans. Medical image segmentation is a crucial technology for automating CT image analysis, significantly accelerating diagnosis and treatment planning. The growing importance of CT in modern medical diagnostics and the need to improve the accuracy and efficiency of image analysis underscore the relevance of this topic. Segmenting internal organs in CT scans holds the potential to enhance diagnostic precision, surgical planning, and disease monitoring. The study aimed to analyze existing segmentation algorithms for implementation in a computer vision-based urology decision support system. The objectives included: investigating CT image segmentation methods and selecting a tool capable of recognizing the right and left kidneys and identifying the presence of stones within them. Furthermore, this tool should enable the calculation of stone size and mass, and the construction of a density distribution map throughout the stone’s volume. Various image segmentation methods were analyzed, implemented programmatically, and tested on human internal organ CT scans. However, the segmentation methods implemented in this study did not yield satisfactory results for identifying kidneys and kidney stones. Instead, detection algorithms were applied within the urology decision support system, demonstrating good performance in identifying kidney objects, the presence of stones within the kidneys, and allowing for the calculation of the detected stones’ parameters.

title: Molecular Filter Design for DNA Molecular Communication System
authors: Wenlong Yu, Lin Lin
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) has emerged as one of the most promising technologies for micro/nanoscale communication. DNA has drawn significant attention for its potential in enabling MC. Current research often uses DNA probes to detect DNA signal molecule concentrations, but this detection method suffers from two major limitations. Firstly, detecting DNA molecules consumes probes in equal amounts, severely limiting the long-term sustainability of the system. Secondly, the decision-making process following detection relies on manual intervention, preventing autonomous operation. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents a novel molecular filter that integrates DNA strand displacement reactions (SDR) with nickase-catalyzed reactions. The core mechanism involves: (1) Signal-triggered SDR forming nickase-recognizable double-strand DNA (dsDNA), and (2) Enzymatic cleavage regenerating reusable components. The cyclic occurrence of these two steps can achieve continuous filtering of signal molecules. The simulation results demonstrate that this filter effectively eliminates interference and serves as an optimal threshold decision device under specific conditions. In particular, it reduces the probe consumption by 50% while reducing the bit error rate of system. This innovative molecular filter paves the way for more reliable and resource-efficient DNA-based MC systems.

title: Review of Flow and Heat Transfer Characteristics in Cryogenic Propellant Filling Process
authors: Chunhao Yang, Chunzhi Wu, Dong Wang, Yufeng Guo, Xiaopeng Liang, Zhaohui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Safe and efficient propellant filling is the cornerstone to ensure the launch, attitude control, maneuvering and on-orbit operation of the rocket. In-depth research on the flow and heat transfer characteristics of cryogenic propellant filling process is beneficial to enhance fuel quality, improve mission efficiency and payload of rocket and spacecraft, and meet the long-term needs of aerospace engineering. This research reviews the current research status of flow and heat transfer in the cryogenic propellant filling process from the perspective of engineering thermophysics, using basic theories, environmental parameters, and supercritical fluids (SCF) as entry points, and explores further development directions. At present, some experimental conditions, mathematical models and numerical simulations can reflect the filling process of cryogenic propellant under various conditions to a certain extent. The mechanism research and situation simulation of cryogenic fluid under various conditions (such as vibration, microgravity, supercritical, etc.) using engineering thermophysics theory and test conditions, matching calibration of control systems under multiple conditions, and deep supercooling filling are still the focus of future research on the flow and heat transfer characteristics of cryogenic propellant filling processes.

title: Smart House Price Prediction: Evaluating Crime Rates and Employment Trends Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Rakeshreddy, Payamvineesha, Kambleavinash, K.Bhasker Reddy, Mukta Jagdish
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: This study proposes a Flask-based machine learning system to improve house price prediction by incorporating crime rates and employment trends — two critical socioeconomic factors often neglected in existing models. The key contribution of this work lies in integrating these external indicators into a Random Forest-based prediction pipeline, significantly enhancing accuracy over traditional attribute-based models. By deploying the system as an interactive web tool, the study also bridges the gap between predictive analytics and real-world usability, enabling dynamic, real-time price estimations for diverse stakeholders.

title: Energy Storage Optimization for Risk Mitigation in Distribution Networks with Distributed Generation
authors: Lingtong Guo, Jinfu Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: The integration of distributed generation into distribution networks significantly alters power flow distribution, resulting in risks of bus voltage violations and branch overloading. These operational risks can be effectively mitigated through the deployment of energy storage systems. In this paper, an optimization method for energy storage allocation is proposed to suppress operational risks. A multi-objective model is formulated to minimize voltage violation risk, branch overloading risk, and total storage capacity. The model is solved using particle swarm optimization, and the optimal deployment scheme is determined through the entropy weight-TOPSIS method. Case studies are conducted on a modified IEEE 33-bus distribution system to evaluate storage requirements and the effectiveness of the proposed strategy. Simulation results demonstrate that voltage violations risks are reduced by over 94%, and overloading risks are nearly eliminated. This paper provides valuable references for the operation of advanced distribution networks.

title: ZhiSync: Metadata-Driven Inference Fusion for Decentralized Healthcare IoT
authors: Anshita Gupta, Sudip Misra
abstract: Heterogeneous healthcare IoT edge devices operate in isolation. This limits their ability to make reliable collaborative decisions in real-time clinical settings. Existing systems lack lightweight inference-fusion mechanisms for decentralized coordination across sensors. In this work, we propose ZhiSync, a plugand-play, metadata-driven collaborative inference framework for heterogeneous healthcare IoT systems. In this system, we allow the devices to deploy their lightweight neural models for modality-specific prediction. We develop a framework that allows devices to exchange confidence, urgency, and timestamp metadata, known as ZhiTag, over asynchronous User Datagram Protocol (UDP). We deploy an inference algorithm, ZhiAware, which integrates peer ZhiTag with local input to adaptively refine predictions in real-time. We test our system on several parameters using a simulation framework with real datasets that includes three devices, namely, an Electrocardiogram (ECG) monitor, a motion sensor, and a breath analyzer, using embedded neural networks. ZhiSync increases local prediction confidence in 99.6% of decisions, with an average relative gain of 20.6% and stronger effects under high-urgency events and reduces false positives. CPU RAM and runtime is less than 2% as compared to baseline. Communication remains sub-kilobyte per second per node. Overall, lightweight metadata exchange shows that ZhiSync provides a reliable and scalable collaboration across decentralized healthcare IoT.

title: Efficient Task Offloading via Semi-Matching for Energy Harvesting D2D Communications
authors: Xuanke Jiang, Sherief Hashima, Kohei Hatano, Eiji Takimoto
abstract: In this article, we investigate the joint task offload for Device-to-Device (D2D) communications with energy harvesting and power control requirements. Utilizing D2D communication for data offloading effectively decreases the load on cellular Base Stations (BSs). Thus, we investigate the challenge of optimizing connection density in D2D networks with multiple connections and investigate scenarios involving delays and energy harvesting, aiming for simultaneous transmissions and efficient resource utilization accordingly. The BS assigns each device a task, and the under-resourced devices aim to build a connection with one helper and offload part of the task. This scheduling policy includes optimal task assignment and practical helper choice. We formulate this problem as finding a semi-matching over a bipartite graph derived from multiple connections and distinct constraints, i.e., power budget and time constraint. Furthermore, we compared our proposed solution, Energy Harvesting Task Offloading Semi-Matching (EHTO-SM), with predefined baselines. Numerical results confirm that the proposed task allocation scheme provides users with high-quality services and demonstrates the effectiveness and dynamic resource adaptability in multiuser settings in various scenarios.

title: Theoretical Limits of Differential Doppler Positioning Using LEO Satellite Signals
authors: Qamar Bader, Sharief Saleh, Gonzalo Seco-Granados, Aboelmagd Noureldin
abstract: As the need for more accurate and reliable positioning systems grows, satellite-based navigation techniques are gaining significant attention, particularly those utilizing Doppler shifts from Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites. Traditional Doppler positioning systems often suffer from errors induced by atmospheric disturbances, satellite clock biases, and other signal impairments, especially in dynamic environments. This has motivated the exploration of differential Doppler positioning as a promising solution to mitigate these common-mode errors. This paper explores the theoretical limits of differential Doppler positioning, focusing on Doppler-only methods where position and velocity estimates are derived from Doppler measurements without relying on time-of-arrival (TOA) measurements. By leveraging the Cramér-Rao lower bound (CRLB), we provide a theoretical performance benchmark for the accuracy of position, velocity, and frequency bias estimation. Furthermore, we present a correlation model for atmospheric effects to demonstrate the impact of baseline distance on the estimation performance of differential Doppler positioning. The results show that differential Doppler positioning notably outperforms traditional non-differential Doppler positioning, particularly in low-SNR environments, with substantial gains in frequency bias, 3D velocity, and 3D position estimation accuracy.

title: Fault Ride-Through Method for Flexible MVDC Electrified Railway Power Supply System Based on Feeder Fault Current Limiting
authors: Yunjiang Liu, Haitao Hu, Xiaoli Wang, Ye Gang, Qiao Ran, Ruijin Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Evolution and Power Engineering: Transition, Intelligence, and Autonomy (EEPE-TIA)
abstract: Flexible medium-voltage DC electrified railway power supply system has attracted extensive research interest because of its advantages, such as long power supply distances and high flexibility. However, the weak damping and continuous structure of the traction network in this system lead to high fault current and extensive impact areas during faults. To address these challenges, this paper first presented a segmented scheme for medium-voltage DC traction network, including high-voltage switchgear configuration. Furthermore, a thyristor-based multi-port fault current limiter (FCL) is proposed. The circuit topology, operation principle, and parameter design of the FCL are introduced. The FCL can effectively suppress the rise rate and peak value of the feeder current following a traction network short-circuit fault. In this way, the locking of traction substation converters can be avoided, ultimately enabling system fault ride-through (FRT). With FRT, trains in non-faulted sections can still operate normally after a single point fault occurs. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified via simulation tests.

title: Optimizing Traffic Management Through Genetic Algorithm
authors: Vineet Panwar, Madhumita Mahapatra, Seema Verma, Deepika Khurana
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: This paper focuses on one of the major challenges of modern day & which is very common nowadays i.e., urban traffic congestion, which is a major obstacle for efficient transportation, safety and yes, human life itself. Traditional traffic control systems which are used for traffic management rely on red and green light timing, which is controlled by historical data, not current found conditions. That leads to problems like congestion at intersections, drivers waiting unnecessarily, increased fuel consumption and emissions from idling vehicles, and increased safety concerns as vehicles aren't very responsive to changes like emergency vehicles arriving. To address these challenges, researchers have devised an innovative traffic management system by using Genetic Algorithm. In this paper AI technology has been used for traffic density analysis as well as object detection. At the same time emphasis has been given to detect vehicles and also prioritization of emergency vehicles. This model uses an advanced object tracking along with adapted traffic density control to adapt to real time traffic conditions. It correctly identifies and tracks different types of vehicles including two wheelers, three wheelers, four wheelers, and emergency vehicles by using the data from traffic camera feeds. The efficiency of this AI based traffic control system in terms of travel time reduction and transportation efficiency in the urban case has been done through extensive experimentation and validation. On the development of intelligent transportation systems, this research involves dynamically adjusting traffic signal timings and lane assignments. The different findings carry significance in urban planning and sustainable development processes and are useful for the development of future smart traffic management solutions.

title: Audit 5.0: The Convergence of Technological Innovation and Human Purpose in the Digital Age
authors: Maria C Tavares, Maria F. R. Almeida, José Vale
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: Financial auditing has undergone continuous evolution, driven by technological, regulatory, and social changes. From its manual origins to the Artificial Intelligence (AI) era, auditing reflects the growing need to meet demands for transparency, accuracy, and efficiency. Accordingly, this paper aims to analyze the evolution of financial auditing, with particular emphasis on the 4.0 and 5.0 phases, stressing the transformative role of digital technologies—especially AI—and the importance of a human-centered approach. A qualitative methodology was employed, based on an up-to-date narrative literature review, to explore the evolutionary milestones of auditing and the challenges and opportunities related to its digital transformation. The study contributes to the understanding of contemporary auditing as a hybrid process that integrates technological capabilities with human judgment, proposing a model that emphasizes ethics, sustainability, and resilience. The growing relevance of AI is reflected in its ability to enhance efficiency, detect fraud, and perform predictive analysis. Audit 5.0 emerges as a response to the need for balancing automation with human values and ethical responsibility.

title: Synergistic Gain for OTFS/AFDM Multi-Satellite Transmission System
authors: Yuqi Huang, Xinyue Ren, Lin Mei, Ye Wang, Qinyu Zhang
abstract: The thriving of satellite communication (SatCom), particularly the expansion of constellation size, offers significant opportunities for cooperative multi-satellite transmission (MST). MST leverages the diversity of fading channels arising from the spatial separation of satellites through novel waveform schemes, such as delay-Doppler (DD) domain-based orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) and chirp domain-based affine Fourier division multiplexing (AFDM). This paper demonstrates that MST can achieve substantial synergistic gain by combining signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) gain and diversity gain with proper waveform design. We prove that OTFS/AFDM avoids the loss of diversity distinguishability compared to current orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) and single-carrier (SC) systems, thereby maximizing the synergistic gain of MST. Furthermore, our simulation results indicate that, although the maximum likelihood (ML) receiver can theoretically achieve the SNR gain of MST under weak small-scale fading channels, the MMSE equalizer fails to do so. Overall, the results suggest that MST is more suitable for severe fading channels and highlight a challenge for future receiver designs to achieve SNR gain under weak fading channels.

title: ReLoad: Revenue and Load Balancing Aware DRL-based Task Offloading Framework for Vehicular Cloud-Edge Networks
authors: Soojeong Bang, Meejeong Lee
abstract: In next-generation vehicular networks (5G/6G), efficiently offloading computationally intensive tasks to edge/cloud servers is critical to ensuring low latency and scalable service delivery. However, existing solutions often overlook the revenue concerns of service providers and rely on static, region-limited load balancing. In this paper, we propose ReLoad, a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based task offloading framework that jointly maximizes service provider revenue and ensures dynamic load balancing. Our scheme leverages cloud-managed, resource-aware clustering of edge servers, allowing adaptive workload distribution even under heavy traffic fluctuations. Within each cluster, a DRL agent with attention-based feature extraction decides whether to offload tasks to an edge server or the cloud, thereby balancing computation costs, communication overhead, and user payments. Simulation results show that ReLoad achieves up to 10% higher task success rates and 20% greater profitability compared to existing approaches, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-time vehicular offloading for 5G/6G edge environments.

title: Age of Information in Discrete-Time Multisource IoT Wireless Status Updating System with Generic Random Update Transmission Times
authors: Tianci Zhang, Aobo Liu, Zhengchuan Chen, Zhong Tian, Min Wang, Yonghui Li, Tony Q. S. Quek
abstract: This work investigates the information freshness in an Internet of Things wireless status updating system with the generic random update transmission times (UTTs). The considered system is extracted as the multi-source Ber/G/1/1 preemptive queueing model. We pay attention to the statistical characteristics of the age of information (AoI). By employing the Markov multi-dimensional age process method, the analytical expressions of the distribution and average of the AoI are established. The AoI statistical characteristics in the cases of geometrically, negative-binomially, and deterministically distributed UTTs are specified. When multiple sources generate updates simultaneously, one of them can be probabilistically selected to transmit. For improving the overall information freshness, the update selection probabilities (USPs) are optimized to minimize the sum of the average AoIs of sources. We focus on the two-source case and find the closed-form optimal USPs. The numerical results validate the correctness of theoretical analyses and present the effectiveness of USP optimization in enhancing the overall information freshness.

title: A Novel Arc-Suppression Method for Grounding Faults in Distribution Networks Based on Flexible Control of Phase Voltage
authors: Zhongqiang Zhou, Jianwei Ma, Yuan Wen, Xiaofang Liu, Jupeng Zeng, Jie Zhuang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Evolution and Power Engineering: Transition, Intelligence, and Autonomy (EEPE-TIA)
abstract: To address the challenges of achieving full compensation with passive arc-suppression techniques, limited zero-sequence voltage regulation capability, and the poor economic efficiency of active arc-suppression technologies in distribution networks, a novel grounding fault arc-suppression method based on flexible fault-phase voltage regulation is proposed. This method regulates the output current of a controllable current source and switches the corresponding fault-phase switch to inject a zero-sequence current, thereby clamping the fault-phase voltage to zero to achieve reliable arc suppression for grounding faults. The active injection arc-suppression approach via the fault phase offers high installation flexibility and can reduce the capacity and cost of the active arc-suppression device. Furthermore, a current closed-loop control strategy based on a first-order inertial regulation is designed. By optimizing the parameters of the damping resistance and the first-order inertial controller, good dynamic and steady-state performance of the control system is ensured, enabling fast and accurate tracking of the current reference value. Simulations under various operating and fault conditions in the PSIM environment verify that the proposed active injection arc-suppression structure and control method can achieve rapid and reliable arc suppression for grounding faults, and are suitable for different grounding methods and various types of grounding faults.

title: Neurophysiological Characteristics of Adaptive Reasoning for Creative Problem-Solving Strategy
authors: Jun-Young Kim, Young-Seok Kweon, Gi-Hwan Shin, Seong-Whan Lee
abstract: Adaptive reasoning enables humans to flexibly adjust inference strategies when environmental rules or contexts change, yet its underlying neural dynamics remain unclear. This study investigated the neurophysiological mechanisms of adaptive reasoning using a card-sorting paradigm combined with electroencephalography and compared human performance with that of a multimodal large language model. Stimulus-and feedback-locked analyses revealed coordinated delta-theta-alpha dynamics: early delta-theta activity reflected exploratory monitoring and rule inference, whereas occipital alpha engagement indicated confirmatory stabilization of attention after successful rule identification. In contrast, the multimodal large language model exhibited only short-term feedback-driven adjustments without hierarchical rule abstraction or genuine adaptive reasoning. These findings identify the neural signatures of human adaptive reasoning and highlight the need for brain-inspired artificial intelligence that incorporates oscillatory feedback coordination for true context-sensitive adaptation.

title: Resilient Routing for Satellite-Terrestrial Integrated Networks via Cascaded Two-Time-Scale Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yang Cao, Shao-Yu Lien, Ying-Chang Liang, Xiaohu Tang
abstract: LEO satellites enable global communications beyond terrestrial landform limitations, but their dynamic mobility disrupts the symmetric and regular inter-satellite connections needed for conventional end-to-end routing designs. To mitigate potential disruptions and detours, terrestrial optical networks can be utilized to provide more resilient connections between satellites/users. However, such satellite-terrestrial integrated networks (STINs) involving massive heteogeneous relay nodes (RNs) creates more complex connectivity and traffic variations, demanding intelligent and proactive routing mechanisms to optimize the overall performance. In this paper, we propose a cascaded two-time-scale deep reinforcement learning scheme for enhancing routing resilience of STINs, which decomposes the overall performance optimization involving multiple space and terrestrial RNs into a series of two-agent two-time-scale Markov decision processes for two successive RNs. Through estimating the average reward based on delayed responses from packet-delivery successors, RNs as DRL agents can proactively estimate their decision-making contributions so as to optimize the overall performance instead of passively waiting for long-horizon backward induction procedures. Simulation results indicate that the proposed scheme can achieve over 20% higher rewards than learning based benchmarks in the high traffic load circumstance and attain near-optimal overall latency under dynamic connectivity patterns.

title: RapidAtt: A Fast Attestation Technique for Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Syed Owais Athar, Muhammad Naveed Aman, Biplab Sikdar
abstract: The industrial Internet of things (IIoT) relies on programmable logic controllers (PLCs) for critical operations, therefore making them prime targets for cyber-attacks especially when the program is manipulated with malevolent intent. Current attestation methods either need ongoing monitoring of the PLC program during runtime which results in substantial computational burden, or rely on physical models that are challenging to accurately develop and maintain with precision. This paper introduces a novel and efficient attestation method exclusively developed for IIoT settings, which effectively combines efficiency and security, particularly in legacy PLCs that may not have sufficient computing capabilities. Contrary to continuous attestation, this approach conducts periodic attestation at intervals and selectively validates different parts of the PLC program randomly against the legitimate PLC program. Implementing this focused strategy decreases the computational load while ensuring a strong probability of detecting unauthorized modifications. Experimental verification demonstrates that our approach achieves a total verification time of 11.93 ms (i.e. improving execution time by up to 17.67% over existing techniques), and maintains detection accuracy above 90%, thereby offering superior efficiency and security for both contemporary and older Programmable Logic Controllers in industrial environments.

title: Indoor-Office Large-Scale Wireless Channel Characterization in cmWave/FR3 Spectrum
authors: Ojas Kanhere, Karl. F. Nieman, Saeed S. Ghassemzadeh
abstract: This paper presents comprehensive findings on the characterization of Indoor Hotspot channel parameters, derived from an extensive experimental campaign conducted at 6.9, 8.3, and 14.5 GHz in a commercial office building. Extensive measurements were carried out in diverse indoor office settings, including cubicles, conference rooms, hallways, and laboratory spaces across four floors. The path loss, shadow fading, delay spread, and angular spread was modeled. Our results offer significant insights into the attenuation and dispersion characteristics of wireless signals in diverse indoor settings in the centimeter-wave frequency band, and can be used for improving indoor network design and performance in commercial buildings.

title: Study on the Discrepancy Between Real-World and Certified Driving Range of New Energy Vehicles in Low and High-Temperature Environments
authors: Yongren Zhang, Kunqi Ma, Fengbin Wang, Junyu Yi, Yang Wang, Ping Yu, Yu Liu, Hanzhengnan Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: This paper addresses the significant discrepancy between the Certified driving range and the Real-world driving range of new energy vehicles under high and low-temperature conditions. Based on the energy flow during real-world vehicle operation, a quantitative analysis of the causes for this disparity was conducted. The study separately analyzes the differences between user's actual driving conditions and the CLTC-P (China Light-duty vehicle Test Cycle-Passenger) standards, the impact of varying road friction resistance at different temperatures on driving range, the influence of air conditioning energy consumption in high and low temperatures, the effect of intelligent driving systems on energy consumption, and the impact of user habits on the driving range. The results indicate that increased resistance at low temperatures, air conditioning for cooling/heating, and the use of intelligent driving functions are the primary reasons for the reduction in driving range compared to the Certified values. Specifically, under low-temperature conditions, air conditioning for heating contributes to approximately a 25.64% decrease in driving range, while intelligent driving/infotainment systems account for a 9.09% reduction. Increased resistance at low temperatures leads to a 6.1% decrease, and other factors (such as battery discharge efficiency, transmission efficiency, etc.) contribute to a 1.37% reduction. Under high-temperature conditions, air conditioning for cooling results in a 9.24% decrease in driving range, intelligent driving/infotainment systems cause a 9.09% decrease, and other factors account for a 2.07% reduction. The findings of this research are of significant importance for helping consumers to objectively understand the issue of driving range degradation and for alleviating driver's range anxiety.

title: Row and Group Pseudo Sparsity-Aware Bayesian Learning for MIMO-OTFS Channel Estimation
authors: Kengo Furuta, Takumi Takahashi, Hideki Ochiai
abstract: This paper proposes a novel sparse Bayesian learning (SBL) algorithm for channel estimation (CE) in multiple-input multiple-output orthogonal time frequency space (MIMO-OTFS) systems, aiming to exploit both the row and group (RG) sparsity and pseudo-sparsity inherent in practical delay– Doppler (DD)-domain channels. Conventional CE approaches model the DD-domain OTFS channel as strictly sparse; however, due to fractional delay and Doppler shifts, the DD-domain channel observed in practical bases does not exhibit strict sparsity, even if the original impulse response is sparse. Instead, it exhibits a structure characterized by numerous small but nonzero elements, with significant variations in signal strength across entries. Capturing this pseudo-sparse structure is crucial for further performance improvement. To address this, we design a new prior distribution that integrates a heavy-tailed complex t-distribution, which effectively captures pseudo-sparsity, with RG sparsity to model the clustered sparse patterns inherent in MIMO-OTFS channels. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art (SotA) sparse signal recovery (SSR)-based alternatives in terms of both CE accuracy and data detection performance.

title: Research on High-Impedance Ground Fault Detection Method in PV Distribution Network Based on Zerosequence Current Characteristics
authors: Lei Yao, Zhenggang Wang, Hongjun Zhang, Zhengqing Qiao, Peng Shen, Fu Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Evolution and Power Engineering: Transition, Intelligence, and Autonomy (EEPE-TIA)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of photovoltaic (PV) power stations into distribution networks, traditional relay protection faces new challenges in detecting high-impedance faults (HIF). HIF is often accompanied by arc discharge, exhibiting weak fault current and inconspicuous features that are difficult to be detected by conventional protection devices. Especially in PV-integrated distribution networks with multiple power sources, the characteristics of fault current become more complex, reducing the adaptability of traditional detection methods. This paper establishes a distribution network model including PV power stations based on the PSCAD/EMTDC simulation platform, and thoroughly analyzes the time-domain and frequency-domain characteristics of the zero-sequence current under HIF conditions. On this basis, a time-domain analysis method based on the concavity and convexity of the second derivative of the zero-sequence current and a frequencydomain analysis method based on the amplitude and phase characteristics of the third harmonic are proposed, constructing fault criteria from both the dynamic variation and harmonic structure of the fault current. The algorithms are tested and verified on both the system side and the PV side. The results show that the proposed method can effectively identify arcinduced high-impedance ground faults in PV-integrated distribution networks, demonstrating strong anti-interference capability and adaptability. It provides a new approach and methodology for addressing the challenges of high-impedance fault detection in distribution networks with PV integration.

title: Spread Spectrum-based Optimization of Physical Layer Security in Elastic Optical Networks
authors: Giannis Savva, Konstantinos Manousakis, Georgios Ellinas
abstract: A spread spectrum-based (SS) technique is utilized to provide physical layer security in elastic optical networks. Specifically, an integer linear program (ILP) formulation is developed that solves the routing and slot allocation problem in conjunction with the spread spectrum technique. Performance results indicate that the proposed technique, in conjunction with signal overlapping that allows the combination of resources used by different connections on the same slots, provides security to the requested connections while at the same time significantly minimizes the additional requirement in network resources.

title: Sub-Centimeter Indoor Optical Wireless Positioning Using An Optimized Machine Learning Technique
authors: Hossien B. Eldeeb, Othman I. Younus, Sina Babadi, Isaac N. O. Osahon, Rizwana Ahmad, Iman Tavakkolnia, Harald Haas
abstract: This paper proposes a novel indoor optical wireless positioning (IOWP) framework that aims to enhance localization precision and robustness through an advanced machine learning (ML)-driven fusion technique. Unlike traditional single-model approaches, the proposed framework uses received signal strength (RSS) data to intelligently combine multiple lightweight ML algorithms, including K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest (RF), and Gaussian Process Regression (GPR). In the training phase, our system utilizes a performance-optimized weight allocation strategy to identify the optimal weights, harnessing the complementary strengths of individual models while mitigating their limitations to achieve exceptional generalization in complex indoor environments. A comprehensive evaluation is conducted under a realistic ray-traced channel model that incorporates typical light distributions, high-order multipath reflections from walls and objects, and mixed diffuse-reflective surface interactions. Performance is assessed in terms of mean positioning error (MPE), 90th percentile (P90) error, and computational complexity. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an MPE of 0.5 cm and a P90 error below 1 cm, offering a practical and scalable solution for next-generation IOWP applications in smart environments.

title: Deep Unfolding-Aided Tensor Decomposition for mmWave MIMO-OFDM Channel Estimation
authors: Shoma Hara, Takumi Takahashi, Hideki Ochiai
abstract: This paper proposes a deep unfolding (DU)-aided channel estimation (CE) method based on tensor decomposition for millimeter-wave (mmWave) multiple-input multiple-output orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (MIMO-OFDM) systems. Since mmWave MIMO channels are composed of only a few propagation paths, they inherently exhibit low-rank characteristics. This property enables CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (CP) decomposition to attain high estimation accuracy while maintaining low pilot overhead. However, when the number of paths (i.e., the tensor rank) is unknown, the low-rank tensor imputation (LRTI) algorithm, which performs CP decomposition, faces the drawback of requiring a large number of iterations to achieve convergence. To address this issue, we investigate the application of DU to LRTI, aiming to achieve accurate CP decomposition even under a limited number of iterations. Specifically, we enhance the convergence rate of LRTI by optimizing the regularization parameters. Additional performance gains are achieved by integrating a well-designed joint loss function tailored to LRTI-based CE into the signal flow graph during the training phase. Computer simulations demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves CE accuracy with fewer iterations.

title: Joint Optimization of Feedback Signal Transmission and Reconstruction in Tactile Internet
authors: Hao Xiang, Tong Zhang, Jiayuan Chen, Changyan Yi
abstract: This paper proposes a novel multi-objective Bayesian optimization approach for tactile feedback transmission and reconstruction in network function virtualization-based Tactile Internet (NFV-based TI). For such a system, guaranteeing real-time and precise feedback transmission and reconstruction is crucial for achieving seamless remote interactions. By jointly optimizing virtual network function (VNF) placement, routing, service function chain (SFC) admission control and wireless resource allocation, this work addresses dynamic adjustment strategies under uncertain network conditions. We propose a multi-objective Bayesian approach tailored for hybrid black-white box optimization problems. A hybrid kernel surrogate model along with adaptive sampling strategies are designed to handle the uncertainties in NFV-based TI environments. This enables Pareto-optimal trade-offs between feedback delay and fidelity. Simulations confirm the superiority of our approach over existing solutions.

title: Next-Generation Sustainable Wireless Systems: Energy Efficiency Meets Environmental Impact
authors: Christo Kurisummoottil Thomas, Omar Hashash, Kimia Ehsani, Walid Saad
abstract: Aligning with the global mandates pushing towards advanced technologies with reduced resource consumption and environmental impacts, the sustainability of wireless networks becomes a significant concern in 6G systems. To address this concern, a native integration of sustainability into the operations of next-generation networks through novel designs and metrics is necessary. Nevertheless, existing wireless sustainability efforts remain limited to energy-efficient network designs which fail to capture the environmental impact of such systems. In this paper, a novel sustainability metric is proposed that captures emissions per bit, providing a rigorous measure of the environmental footprint associated with energy consumption in 6G networks. This metric also captures how energy, computing, and communication resource parameters influence the reduction of emissions per bit. Then, the problem of allocating the energy, computing and communication resources is posed as a multi-objective (MO) optimization problem. To solve the resulting non-convex problem, our framework leverages MO reinforcement learning (MORL) to maximize the novel sustainability metric alongside minimizing energy consumption and average delays in successfully delivering the data, all while adhering to constraints on energy resource capacity. The proposed MORL methodology computes a global policy that achieves a Pareto-optimal tradeoff among multiple objectives, thereby balancing environmental sustainability with network performance. Simulation results show that the proposed approach reduces the average emissions per bit by around 26% compared to state-of-the-art methods that do not explicitly integrate carbon emissions into their control objectives.

title: Machine-Learning Based Artificial Affective Perception For Machines’ Emotional Awareness
authors: Fatemeh Saveh, Mohand Tahar Soualah, Kurosh Madani, Véronique Amarger, Abdennasser Chebira
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: As liable consequence of the rapid expansion of robots’ roles in humans’ everyday activity, providing machines with emotional awareness constitutes an actual challenge for affective computing toward machines-and-humans coexistence. Indeed, for equating humans' awareness robots’ have develop affective understanding of the complex humans’ environment where they are supposed to evolve and interact with them. For this, machines have to not only be equipped with rational computational skills but also acquire emotional (aesthetic) perception regarding their environment, proffering them a kind of human-like self-sufficiency of emotional appraisal of their surroundings.In this paper we present a computational multi-model-based solution for machines’ Affective Aesthetic Artificial Perception (AAAP). Issued from philosophical and psychological foundations relating human’s affective behavior and his aesthetic perception of the environment, the presented AAAP provides a computational approach toward the machines’ and humans’ emotional interaction. Holding its basement from philosophical and psychological foundations, the presented AAAP provides an explainable conceptualization of human-like artificial affective visual perception.Using adequate experimental data sets the paper provides experimental results showing the effectiveness of the investigated model.

title: Learning-Based Channel Estimator with Novel Pilot Design for CF IRS-Aided mMIMO-OTFS Systems
authors: Mudasir Ahmad Sheikh, Nishant Arya, Anupama Rajoriya, Rohit Budhiraja
abstract: We propose a novel channel estimator for an uplink cell-free intelligent reflective surface (IRS)-aided orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) system by introducing a pilot frame that reduces pilot overhead by enabling each access point (AP) to estimate the channels of all IRS elements in a single OTFS frame. The cascaded user-IRS-AP delay-Doppler (DD) channel is modeled as a twisted convolution of the user-IRS and IRS-AP DD channels, which now exhibits twisted convolutional sparsity, and common sparsity due to the shared DD profile across IRS elements. We develop a convolutional Bayesian learning (ConvBL) algorithm, which uses expectation-maximization for calculating channel posterior distribution, and natural gradient descent for optimizing prior parameters. We show that ConvBL vastly outperforms Bayesian and non-Bayesian algorithms.

title: Decentralized Gossip Learning with Adaptive OOD Detection for LEO Satellite Networks under Non-IID Data
authors: Quanwei Zhang, Jianping Pan
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations offer great promise for distributed sensing and intelligence in future networks. However, their potential is hindered by communication inefficiencies and heterogeneous data distributions, which lead to excessive transmission costs, slow convergence, and degraded model performance. Existing federated learning (FL) methods alleviate data privacy and communication load, but typically rely on centralized coordination and lack robustness to Non-IID data, especially in dynamic satellite environments. To address these limitations, we propose the GOOD (Gossip Learning with OOD Enhancement) framework, a fully decentralized, gossip-based FL system enhanced with adaptive out-of-distribution (OOD) detection. GOOD enables satellites to evaluate the distributional compatibility of incoming model updates using lightweight OOD scores, thereby reducing negative transfer and eliminating ineffective transmissions. Additionally, it introduces an adaptive grouping mechanism that dynamically clusters satellites based on distributional similarity, optimizing inter-satellite communication. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GOOD consistently outperforms prior decentralized FL approaches in terms of convergence accuracy and communication efficiency under both statistical and semantic Non-IID conditions, making it a compelling solution for autonomous on-orbit learning.

title: Model-Driven Deep Learning-Aided Wideband Hybrid-Field THz UM-MIMO Channel Estimation
authors: Yuanjian Li, A. S. Madhukumar, Zheng Chu, Miao Zhang, Qian Dong
abstract: To efficiently implement Terahertz (THz) communications in the 6G era, the ultra-massive multiple-input multipleoutput (UM-MIMO) technique is considered an essential building block. However, effective wideband THz UM-MIMO transmissions can never be achieved without pilot-inexpensive yet accurate channel estimation (CE) methods. In this article, we investigate the wideband THz UM-MIMO CE problem, accounting for the hybrid near- and far-field propagation characteristics, molecular absorption, and multi-path reflection. The CE problem is reformulated into a compressed sensing-aided counterpart, leveraging the inherent sparsity of THz UM-MIMO channels to reduce pilot overhead. We harness the power of model-driven deep learning and propose a deep unfolding (DU)-aided Bayesian learning (DUBL) CE algorithm. We tailor the structure of the deep neural network (DNN)-based unfolded expectation-maximization (EM) iteration, aiming to achieve efficient DUBL training performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the DUBL solution can offer substantial THz UM-MIMO CE gains over the considered representative benchmarks.

title: UnSLU-BENCH+: Extended Machine Unlearning Benchmark for Spoken Language Understanding
authors: Claudio Savelli, Alkis Koudounas, Flavio Giobergia, Elena Baralis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: With the growing adoption of voice assistants and Spoken Language Understanding (SLU) systems, the need for machine unlearning, i.e., the ability to remove the influence of specific training data without full retraining, has become increasingly critical to ensure user privacy and regulatory compliance. In this work, we present UnSLU-BENCH+, an extended benchmark for systematically evaluating unlearning methods in SLU. Our benchmark spans six datasets in four languages, each paired with three self-supervised models, and covers eleven state-of-the art unlearning techniques. These methods are evaluated based on three foundational principles of unlearning: utility, i.e., the performance of the unlearned model, efficacy, i.e., the actual removal of the information to be forgotten, and efficiency, i.e., the computational cost. To support this evaluation, we introduce CGUM, a classification-aware extension of the Global Unlearning Metric (GUM), which provides a unified, interpretable score that jointly captures these three dimensions. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that unlearning performance varies significantly across models and datasets, with no single method universally dominating. Our results establish a solid foundation for evaluating and developing future machine unlearning techniques in speech applications while promoting CGUM as a robust evaluation metric for every classification-based unlearning scenario.

title: LoRAT: Low Rank Adaptation and Transfer for Multi-environment Channel Estimations
authors: Qi Wang, Xubo Li, Yong Xiao
abstract: Data-driven AI models, particularly deep learning-based wireless channel estimation solutions, have exhibited promising potentials in modeling the intricate, non-linear relationships between environmental conditions and wireless channel characteristics. However, multi-environment model training requires high computational and communication resource costs. Also, each AI model has limited generality and cannot be directly adopted to new and unknown environments that have different channel conditions, compared to its training dataset. To address these limitations, this paper proposes LoRAT, a low-rank model adaptation and transfer framework for efficient multi-environment channel estimation model development. In LoRAT, a number of foundation models are first trained for a limited number of known environments, and then a simple low-rank model transfer approach is proposed to enable quick and efficient transfer of these foundation models to new unknown environments. We consider the uplink-based downink channel state information (CSI) estimation problem for an FDD wireless system as an example to describe the implementation details and evaluate performance of LoRAT. In this case, we train a set of attention-based diffusion models as foundation models for downlink CSI estimations in the known environments and we show that transferring the low-rank parts of these foundation models is capable of constructing environment-specific CSI estimation models for any new unknown environment. Our proposed LoRAT does not require any labeled datasets in the unknown environments and, since the model transfer only requires to calculate the low-rank part of the model parameters, the computational and communication cost is much lower than the traditional AI model-based solutions, especially for wireless systems across a large number of different environments. Extensive experiments have been conducted to compare the performance of LoRAT with state-of-the-art solutions. Our experimental results suggest that LoRAT provides up to 15% and 265% improvement in accuracy of multi-environment channel estimation, compared to FIRE and codebook-based solution, respectively.

title: Reliable Real-Time Edge AI via Conformal Model Selection
authors: Anders E. Kalør, Tomoaki Ohtsuki
abstract: Edge artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to be a central part of 6G, where servers located at the edge of the network will support devices in performing inference using machine learning (ML) models. However, providing latency and accuracy guarantees needed by many 6G applications, such as automated driving and robotics, is challenging due to the black-box nature of ML models, the complexity of the tasks, and the random wireless channel. This paper proposes a novel framework leveraging conformal risk control to meet requirements on the expected loss under a strict deadline. To adapt to fluctuating channel conditions, our framework utilizes an ensemble of black-box encoder/decoder models and inference models of varying accuracy and complexity, and selects the model expected to yield the most informative prediction under the given requirements. We demonstrate the proposed framework on a deadline-constrained image classification task under a strict missed detection requirement. The results suggest that the proposed framework provides the required performance guarantees, making it a promising step toward achieving reliable real-time edge AI services in 6G.

title: Temperature-Flow Performance of Natural Ester Transformer Oils through Molecular Dynamics Investigation of Viscosity, Pour Point, and Unsaturation Effects
authors: T Jarin, G. Kannayeram, Rathinam Muniraj, Anoopa Jose Chittilappilly, Giby Jose
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This research investigates the temperature–flow response of natural ester transformer oils by molecular dynamics (MD) simulations with emphasis on triglyceride unsaturation effects on viscosity, pour point, and molecular packing. The systems are simulated for three triglycerides: tristearin (C18:0, saturated), triolein (C18:1, monounsaturated), and trilinolein (C18:2, polyunsaturated). Simulations use the AMBER-Lipid14 force field and Green-Kubo formalism to compute viscosity from 233–373 K. Results indicate that increase in unsaturation lowers dynamic and kinematic viscosities and pour points. At 298 K, dynamic viscosity drops from 340 ± 12 mPa·s (tristearin) to 285 ± 9 mPa·s (triolein) and 245 ± 8 mPa·s (trilinolein), corresponding to kinematic viscosities of 370 ± 13, 320 ± 10, and 281 ± 9 cSt , respectively. Experimentally measured pour points are 259 K (–14 °C) for tristearin, 268 K (–5 °C) for triolein, and 253 K (–20 °C) for trilinolein. The simulations reproduce the relative reduction in pour point with increasing unsaturation. Molecular analysis shows that unsaturation introduces chain bends, leading to packing interruptions, 17% increase in free volume, and higher diffusion coefficients (2.1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–11</sup> to 2.7×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–1</sup>1m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/s). As a result, activation energies for viscous flow reduce from 45.2 ± 1.1 to 38.9 ± 0.9 kJ/mol. Simulation data are in good agreement with experimental data. The results provide molecular-level insights into the design of ester transformer oils with better low-temperature performance.

title: Tradeoffs Between Resilience, Safety, and String Stability in Vehicle Platoons With Feasibility-Aware Control Barrier Functions
authors: Ruijie Ma, Chengcheng Zhao, Lin Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article investigates how to achieve resilience, safety, and string stability simultaneously for vehicle platoons under false data injection (FDI) attacks. We utilize the resilient control barrier function-quadratic programming (CBF-QP) framework to formulate the control objective. Specifically, a network-redundancy-based resilient controller serves as the nominal controller to ensure internal stability, while safety and string stability are characterized by control barrier function (CBF) constraints for control inputs optimization. We introduce a novel concept of boundary robustness to preserve the validity of CBF constraints by incorporating a robustness margin under bounded attacks. As strict enforcement of multiple CBFs may cause controller infeasibility, we improve quadratic programming (QP) feasibility via parameter predesign and a feasibility-CBF with bounded adaptive relaxation. Our analysis reveals conflicts among resilience, safety, and string stability, and we propose a conditional activation-relaxation approach to achieve tradeoffs among them. Comparative simulations and experiments involving four TurtleBot3s are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Underdetermined Blind Source Separation via Weighted Simplex Shrinkage Regularization and Quantum Deep Image Prior
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin, Si-Sheng Young
abstract: As most optical satellites remotely acquire multispectral images (MSIs) with limited spatial resolution, multispectral unmixing (MU) becomes a critical signal processing technology for analyzing the pure material spectra for high-precision classification and identification. Unlike the widely investigated hyperspectral unmixing (HU) problem, MU is much more challenging as it corresponds to the underdetermined blind source separation (BSS) problem, where the number of sources is larger than the number of available multispectral bands. In this article, we transform MU into its overdetermined counterpart (i.e., HU) by inventing a radically new quantum deep image prior (QDIP), which relies on the virtual band-splitting task conducted on the observed MSI for generating the virtual hyperspectral image (HSI). Then, we perform HU on the virtual HSI to obtain the virtual hyperspectral sources. Though HU is overdetermined, it still suffers from the ill-posed issue, for which we employ the convex geometry structure of the HSI pixels to customize a weighted simplex shrinkage (WSS) regularizer to mitigate the ill-posedness. Finally, the virtual hyperspectral sources are spectrally downsampled to obtain the desired multispectral sources. The proposed geometry/quantum-empowered MU (GQ-μ) algorithm can also effectively obtain the spatial abundance distribution map for each source, where the geometric WSS regularization is adaptively and automatically controlled based on the sparsity pattern of the abundance tensor. Simulation and real-world data experiments demonstrate the practicality of our unsupervised GQ-μ algorithm for the challenging MU task. Ablation study demonstrates the strength of QDIP, not achieved by classical DIP, and validates the mechanics-inspired WSS geometry regularizer. The associated code will be available at https://github.com/IHCLab/GQ-mu.

title: A Seizure Warning System based on Multidimensional Attention Entropy and Improved Binary Mantis Search Algorithm
authors: Duanpo Wu, Shuchang Zhang, Yi Lin, Yuhan Gao, Tiejia Jiang, Yixuan Yuan, Xiaolong Ye, Chenggang Yan
abstract: Seizure warning system (SWS) based on electroencephalography (EEG) is a prominent research focus in the internet of medical things (IoMT). An effective SWS enables epilepsy patients to proactively implement interventions before seizures. Effective feature representation of EEG reduces communication load from IoMT processing devices to cloud servers and enhances subsequent machine/deep learning classification performance. This paper proposes a seizure prediction system based on multidimensional attention entropy (MAE) and an improved binary mantis search algorithm (IBMSA). First, the MAE is calculated at the edge computing gateway by extracting attention entropy (AE) from each subband of single channel (AE-ESSC). Based on this, the algorithm further computes the AE of single channel with multiple subbands (AE-SCMS), the AE of single subband with multiple channels (AE-SSMC) and the AE of the original signal in single channel (AE-OSSC). Second, the IBMSA which is deployed on cloud servers uses a composite S-shaped and V-shaped function (CSVF) and dynamic stage selection strategy (DSSS) to find the optimal feature subset. The proposed algorithm is evaluated on data from the CHB-MIT dataset via leave-one-out cross-validation, with experimental results demonstrating an average sensitivity of 94.74% and a false prediction rate of 0.045 per hour. Additionally, edge deployment testing on Raspberry Pi 4 verifies the lightweight feature of the proposed system.

title: Scalable HMM-Based Map Matching with Adaptive Path-Length Caching and Grid-Based Evaluation: A National-Scale Study on Taxi Probe Data in Thailand
authors: Athispat Phongampai, Apichon Witayangkurn
abstract: The analysis of mobility patterns in public transportation has gained significant attention alongside the increasing trend of urbanization, as evidenced by the substantial volume of Global Positioning System (GPS) trajectories collected. These data offer major benefits to vehicle navigation systems, traffic planning, and pollution monitoring. However, raw GPS data are unsuitable for direct use owing to intrinsic reliability limitations, necessitating map-matching techniques, particularly hidden Markov model (HMM)-based approaches, to align GPS trajectories with road networks. Although distributed computing frameworks enhance scalability, many existing solutions remain restricted within urban-scale deployments and often overlook road continuity in low sampling-rate datasets. This study proposes a scalable HMM-based map-matching algorithm that integrates vehicle heading, favors assignments that remain on the current link, and uses an adaptive path length cache to process large-scale probe data with improved accuracy. The algorithm successfully balances accuracy with computational performance by leveraging Apache Spark for distributed processing. This allows for efficient handling of large datasets while incorporating a route interpolation mechanism to address gaps in network coverage caused by sparse measurement intervals. Additionally, a grid-based evaluation framework based on road features is proposed to assess the performance of the algorithm across diverse road types. Experimental results using real-world taxi probe data from Thailand demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, which achieves average matching accuracies of 99.48% for vehicle-based evaluations and 99.56% for grid-based evaluations. Furthermore, the algorithm significantly optimizes computational resources, reducing total processing time by up to 6.7 times compared to traditional HMMs. This method provides a scalable, accurate, and continuous map-matching performance, making it highly suitable for large-scale mobility analysis and transportation applications.

title: CL-ERDA: Certificateless Recoverable Data Auditing with Corruption Localization in Edge Computing
authors: Yongdong Ding, Dengzhi Liu, Jun Shen, Haowen Tan, Q. M. Jonathan Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Edge computing paradigm deploys infrastructures on users' edge side, enabling data to be stored in edge storage systems. Thus edge computing finds a balance between remote cloud storage with high latency and local storage with limited resources. However, the frequent update of edge data increases the probability of data corrupting. To ensure availability and integrity of edge data without affecting future use, this paper proposes a certificateLess edge recoverable data auditing (CL-ERDA) approach with corruption localization. First, the certificateless network coding signature in CL-ERDA is utilized to generate the homomorphic authenticator, addressing public key certificate management issues while avoiding insecure key escrow problems. Then, CL-ERDA designs a two-phase auditing method, where only file-level aggregation proofs are respond in first phase of external check and the second phase of self-check is launched only when damaged data is detected, reducing communication overhead of the auditing. Moreover, CL-ERDA combines hierarchical check method with batch localization method to achieve locating efficiently all the corrupted data at one time rather than locating only one corrupted data at once. Finally, security analysis shows that the proposed scheme resists various attacks and supports the unforgeability of authenticators. Experimental results demonstrate that CL-ERDA is efficient in auditing and corruption localization.

title: Hierarchical Multimodal Motion Control of Magnetic Pivot-Walking Millirobotic-Grippers for Autonomous Target Acquisition in Complex Terrains_supp5-3675526.mp4
authors: Huaping Wang
abstract: Magnetic soft millirobotic-grippers, equipped with agile pivot-walking motions and adaptive enveloping morph abilities, hold great promise for biomedical target acquisition tasks. However, deploying these millirobotic-grippers in highly constrained, disturbed, and variable terrains to realize multisequence target acquisition and transport tasks remains challenging. Here, we introduce a hierarchical multimodal motion control method for pivot-walking magnetic milliroboticgrippers, which enhances adaptive locomotion capabilities and enables high-precision motion control, facilitating autonomous target acquisition in complex terrains. The millirobotic-gripper utilizes a centrosymmetric three-pivot design, enabling adaptive soft enveloping deformation and robust multimodal locomotion. A hierarchical control architecture is proposed, comprising: 1) an upper-level Event-Based Finite State Machine planner that dynamically orchestrates transitions between motion modes according to environmental feedback and task-specific conditions; and 2) a lower-level Sliding Mode controller integrated with Gaussian Process-based gait parameter optimization, significantly improving motion accuracy and robustness against environmental disturbances. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method allows millirobotic-grippers to efficiently navigate morphing tunnels, leap across gaps exceeding 3 times their body length, accurately follow arbitrary paths with errors less than 5% of their body length, and reliably perform three types of targets grasping and transport. Furthermore, the biomedical application potential of our system is initially validated through ex vivo porcine gastrointestinal tract experiment with ultrasound guidance.

title: Hybrid Near/Far-Field Frequency-Dependent Beamforming via Phase-Time Arrays With Single RF Chain
authors: Yeyue Cai, Meixia Tao, Jianhua Mo, Shu Sun
abstract: Phase-time arrays (PTAs), integrating phase shifters and true-time delays, emerge as a cost-effective and energy-efficient architecture for frequency-dependent beamforming in wideband communications. In this work, we investigate a wideband system in which a base station equipped with a PTA and single RF chain serves multiple near-field and far-field users. The goal is to jointly optimize PTA-based beamforming, subband allocation, and power allocation to maximize overall system performance. To this end, we formulate a system utility maximization problem, which includes sum-rate and geometric mean rate maximization as special cases and is highly non-convex. We first develop a three-step alternating optimization (AO) algorithm that iteratively optimizes the beamforming and resource allocations. To further enhance efficiency, we propose an unsupervised learning-based approach that combines a convolutional neural network, a graph attention network (GAT), and a normalization module with a utility-driven loss and a learnable adjacency initialized from hardware couplings. Simulation results confirm that PTAs strike a superior balance between energy efficiency and spectral efficiency compared with fully-digital and phased array architectures. The proposed GAT achieves AO-level performance with orders-of-magnitude lower computational complexity, i.e., only about 0.1% in our simulations.

title: A Fault Diagnosis Method for Transformer Bushings Based on Feature Optimization Using Random Forest and SVM
authors: Renbin Su, Shenzeng Luo, Jun Xie, Zhi Li, Siyu Chang, Hongtao Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: This study proposes a transformer bushing fault diagnosis method based on a feature-optimized Random ForestSVM model. A 21-dimensional feature set, combining original Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) data and gas ratios, was constructed and normalized to improve data quality. Random Forest was used to rank feature importance, facilitating the selection of key diagnostic features and reducing redundancy. These selected features were then classified using a Support Vector Machine (SVM), effectively integrating feature selection with classification. Experimental results demonstrate that the RFSVM9 model outperforms traditional methods and mainstream machine learning models (MLP, BP, KNN) in diagnostic accuracy, highlighting its efficiency and reliability for transformer fault detection.

title: A Spatiotemporal Load Forecasting Method for Electric Vehicles Based on SSA-BIGRU Attention
authors: Hongxin Huang, Yu Zhang, Jiahao Song
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: In order to improve the accuracy of electric vehicle charging load forecasting, this paper proposes a spatiotemporal forecasting method based on sparrow search algorithm (SSA) to optimize bidirectional gating cycle unit attention mechanism (BIGRU- Attention). Firstly, the SSA algorithm is used to globally optimize the hyper parameters of BIGRU-Attention model to achieve high-precision prediction of key characteristics such as the start time, end time and driving distance of electric vehicles; Then, taking the predicted travel characteristics as the input, the spatiotemporal load forecasting model is constructed to generate the regional electric vehicle charging load curve. The experimental results show that this method has achieved remarkable results in the task of travel feature prediction, and the generated load curve accurately reflects the temporal and spatial distribution of the actual charging behavior, especially accurately captures the load peak characteristics around 13:00 noon. This study provides effective technical support for electric vehicle charging infrastructure planning and grid coordinated dispatching.

title: EEG Correlates of Surprise and Reward Prediction Errors in a Probabilistic Learning Task
authors: Sungu Nam, Seong-Ho Ahn, Kyung Yoon Jung, Sang Jin Jang, Sulgi Kim, Sang Wan Lee, Jaeseung Jeong
abstract: Surprising events and prediction errors are thought to drive learning by signaling discrepancies between expectations and outcomes. Here we examined human electroencephalography (EEG) responses to surprise and reward prediction errors (RPEs) in a probabilistic learning task. We applied computational models to quantify surprise on each trial in two ways: (1) Shannon surprise - the negative log-probability of the observed event (information theoretic surprise), and (2) Bayesian surprise - the belief update magnitude (information gain) for an ideal observer. We also computed signed RPEs to capture the valence of outcome deviations (better or worse than expected). Our results suggest partially dissociable EEG signatures for surprise and RPE. Time-resolved multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA) revealed that Shannon surprise was decodable at earlier latencies (~120-200 ms), whereas Bayesian surprise peaked later (~400-600 ms), consistent with a temporal dissociation between rapid surprise detection and later belief updating. These findings suggest a temporal dissociation between fast, stimulus-driven surprise detection and later cognitive evaluation of outcome discrepancies. Understanding how different forms of surprise and prediction errors are encoded in the brain can advance our knowledge of learning algorithms in humans.

title: Design Simulation of Boost Circuit Based on LTspice
authors: Yuxiao Wu, Aaiming Zhao, Yumeng Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Boost power supplies offer significant advantages over traditional DC power supplies in several aspects, making them a more efficient and flexible solution in modern electronic devices. Boost power supplies enable efficient energy conversion with switching mode to boost voltage through inductive energy storage and energy release. A voltage-adjustable switching power supply circuit is designed for LTspice simulation, and the corresponding results are given.

title: Regional Carbon Emission Prediction Based on Transformer-LSTM
authors: Zhenhuan Xu, Xiaofeng Shen, Xinye Wu, Jijian Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: This study focuses on the issue of carbon emission prediction, aiming to develop a highly accurate prediction model to help address climate change. Using the LSTM and Transformer architectures, an LSTM-Transformer hybrid model was constructed. This model utilizes LSTM to capture the short-term dynamics and long-term dependencies of time series, and leverages the self-attention mechanism of Transformer to effectively handle the complex relationships and long-distance dependencies among input variables. During the model training phase, a large amount of historical data related to carbon emissions covering multiple dimensions such as economic growth, energy structure, and industrial structure was used for learning. Experimental results show that the LSTM-Transformer hybrid model performs exceptionally well in evaluation metrics such as MAE and RMSE, significantly improving the accuracy of carbon emission prediction. This research outcome provides a more effective method for carbon emission prediction, and is expected to provide reliable decision-making basis for policy makers and relevant enterprises when formulating emission reduction strategies and planning, having a positive significance for promoting global carbon emission reduction actions.

title: Blockchain-Driven Video Game with Secure Asset Management
authors: Abhay Pratap Yadav, Seema Verma, Madhumita Mahapatra, Pridhi Arora
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a decentralized blockchain-based gaming platform designed to redefine the user experience in competitive gaming through Web3 technologies. Traditional online games suffer from centralized control, limited asset ownership, and trust issues in competitive environments. In contrast, the proposed solution leverages smart contracts, NFTs, and decentralized architecture to create a transparent, trustless, and rewarding gaming ecosystem. The platform enables users to register, link their crypto wallets, and participate in admin-hosted tournaments of a rock paper-scissors game. Here winners are selected by a dynamic leaderboard system. It automatically rewards NFTs depending on final rankings at the end of each tournament. Admin controls the tournament creation and NFT minting. Players can sell/buy the earned NFTs at an integrated exchange. All major activities of the game like tournament flow, result tracking, NFT ownership are controlled by smart contracts control. Due to the smart contracts control safe transactions takes place without the need for intermediaries, which ensures fairness. The study primarily dedicated to smart contract logic, User interface functionalities and NFT marketplace structure. It also deals all issues related to optimization. Other features of the work are real-time leader board management& secure execution of the game logic. The results of this research demonstrates that blockchain can improve transparency in gaming and its reward systems. It has the capacity to create a new model for competitive decentralized gameplay.

title: Advanced Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Rays via Capsule Networks Integrated with Transformer Architectures and Dynamic Routing Attention Mechanisms
authors: Golla Siddhartha Sai, Kumaran K, Saranya G
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major global health concern, particularly affecting children and the elderly in low-resource settings where access to expert radiologists is limited. Auto- mated chest X-ray (CXR) analysis using deep learning offers a promising solution; however, conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) often fail to preserve spatial hierarchies and contextual dependencies, limiting diagnostic accuracy. To address this challenge, a deep learning framework is introduced that integrates Capsule Networks with Transformer modules aug- mented by Dynamic Routing Attention (DRA), and Transformer blocks for binary classification of chest X-rays into normal and pneumonia cases. Capsule Networks retain part-whole spatial relationships, DRA enhances localized feature prioritization, and Transformer modules capture long-range contextual dependen- cies, resulting in a more robust and interpretable diagnostic system. The model was trained and tested on a publicly available Kaggle dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 8 6 3}$</tex> chest X-rays, standardized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 4} \times \text{6 4}$</tex> grayscale inputs. Experimental results demonstrate that the full hybrid model achieved 83.97% accuracy, 0.8021 precision, and 0.9872 recall, outperforming reduced configurations and providing strong sensitivity to pneumonia cases. These findings establish the proposed approach as a computationally efficient and clinically interpretable solution for pneumonia detection, with potential applications in resource-constrained healthcare environments and opportunities for future extensions to multi- class classification and real-world deployment.

title: Efficient Wireless Video Transmission via Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Token Merging
authors: Xinyi Zhou, Danlan Huang, Zhixin Qi, Liang Zhang, Ting Jiang
abstract: The rapid proliferation of emerging video services has substantially increased the demand for real-time transmission of high-definition video content. However, optimizing the trade-off between video transmission quality and communication bandwidth remains a significant challenge. To address this issue, we propose RAJSCC, a rate-adaptive video deep joint source-channel coding (DeepJSCC) framework. RAJSCC incorporates a spatio-temporal token merging mechanism to aggregate semantically similar tokens, effectively reducing redundancy both within and across frames. Additionally, an adaptive token merging predictor, designed based on simple statistical features of the input videos, enables dynamic rate control at the group of pictures (GoP) level, ensuring smooth and continuous variation in the overall video coding rate. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RAJSCC significantly outperforms traditional video transmission schemes, such as H.264 and H.265 with low-density parity-check (LDPC), as well as existing DeepJSCC methods, in terms of reconstruction quality. More importantly, the proposed adaptive spatio-temporal token merging mechanism reduces bandwidth consumption by 63.5% and computational cost by 13.0%, while incurring only a marginal 1–2 dB degradation in reconstruction quality. These findings highlight the effectiveness of RAJSCC in achieving a superior balance between transmission efficiency and video quality, making it a promising solution for real-time high-definition video communication in bandwidth-constrained environments.

title: English to Sanskrit Translation Using Sequence-to-Sequence with Attention
authors: K. Srinivas, Goli Veera Venkata Naga Durga Vasanti, Inampudi Varshini
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Historical languages like Sanskrit require preservation for linguistic research and cultural heritage. English-to-Sanskrit translation remains challenging due to complex grammar and limited parallel data. This paper presents an English-to-Sanskrit translation system based on a Seq2Seq model with attention. The system achieves a validation accuracy of 99.60% and a BLEU score of 0.889, demonstrating substantial improvement over baseline methods. These results confirm that neural approaches provide reliable, fluent, and grammatically consistent translations for low-resource languages. Furthermore, the real-time translation interface demonstrates the system's practical utility. The findings indicate that modern AI can effectively support cultural preservation, bridge the gap between classical and contemporary language studies, and advance research in low-resource neural machine translation.

title: Impact of Decentralized Finance (DeFi) on Traditional Banking Models: A Blockchain Perspective
authors: Surenthran David, S. Aravinth, Chris Sherin D, Mohamed Issath S, Paul Jebarson G, S. Sevvanthi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
