abstract: Extremely large-scale reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (XL-RISs) have been recognized as a promising technology to enhance the capabilities of communication systems and mitigate severe path loss. However, due to the large size of XL-RISs, simply adopting the deployment strategies of RIS may result in coverage areas with relatively low performance improvement. To investigate the potential contribution of each position on the designated surface, we propose a Shapley value sampling method to evaluate their Shapley values. Based on the approximated Shapley values, we propose a linear-time algorithm to determine the near-optimal XL-RIS deployment in terms of expected total transmission rate. Finally, simulation results show that the proposed Shapley sampling method effectively predicts the contribution of each XL-RIS module in total capacity, and we identify the scenarios where our deployment algorithm outperforms the conventional rectangular XL-RIS with the same number of RIS elements.

title: Simultaneous Tracking of Multiple LEO Satellites with Multibeam Phased Array Ground Station
authors: Xiaoxia Cao, Shaohua Wu, Ye Wang, Su Ma, Lin Mei, Qinyu Zhang
abstract: With tens of thousands of low earth orbit (LEO) satellites to be launched in the near future, phased array antennas are envisioned as attractive candidates for future satellite ground stations due to their ability to generate multiple beams via beamforming network, thus supporting multiple satellites simultaneously. Multi-satellite tracking is of great importance for ensuring link quality in satellite communications. However, it is challenging to simultaneously tracking multiple satellites due to orbital perturbations and interference from other satellite signals. In this paper, we propose a multi-satellite tracking scheme for multibeam phased array ground station communication with LEO satellites, which employs direction of arrival (DOA) measurements of satellite signals to aid the satellite dynamics. First, we establish a tracking model that incorporates the relationship between satellite dynamics and measurement angles. Then, we develop a data fusion-based method for multiple LEO satellites by exploiting the DOA measurements of satellite signals using a phased array antenna. The measured DOA data are associated with the target satellite state and processed using an extended Kalman filter (EKF) to enhance tracking accuracy. The updated satellite position is further integrated into a dynamics model to predict angular information, leading to accurate satellite tracking during measurement gaps. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed tracking scheme achieves a tracking accuracy of within 0.1 degrees in multi-satellite scenarios, significantly improving the tracking accuracy compared to other methods.

title: Leveraging Student Feedback and Data-Driven Modeling to Enhance Academic Outcomes: A Correlational Study in Computing Education
authors: Larry Rutaquio, Jesus Paguigan, Kathleen Dimaano
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: This study investigates how student feedback and data-driven modeling can be utilized to improve academic outcomes in computing education. Using a quantitative correlational design to investigate how course difficulty, delivery mode, and student feedback construct satisfaction, engagement, and perception affect academic performance in undergraduate computing courses at EARIST. A stratified random sample of students across online and in-person modalities provided survey data on feedback constructs via a five-point Likert scale, while institutional records supplied grade outcomes. Descriptive statistics summarized data distributions, and Pearson’s correlation measured bivariate associations. A one-way ANOVA analysis compared feedback and performance across delivery modes, revealing significantly higher satisfaction, engagement, perception, and grades for online courses (p < 0.001). Multiple linear regression modeled academic performance as a function of course difficulty, delivery mode, and feedback dimensions, explaining 64.7% of variance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.647, F = 267.84, p < 0.001), with course quality (β = –3.18, p < 0.001) and online delivery (β = 4.87, p < 0.001) as major predictors. Mediation analysis demonstrated that student feedback partially mediates the relationship between course quality and performance, yielding a combined indirect effect of 60.7% and a total enhancement potential of 86.2%. K-means clustering identified three success profiles: Academic Success, Steady Achievers, and Emerging Stars, highlighting diverse pathways to achievement. Key findings underscore that high-quality course design and robust online delivery, coupled with adaptive, interactive, and feedback-driven strategies, significantly enhance student success.

title: Wi-Fi Fine Timing Measurement in Action: Simulation of Performance Trade-Offs in Real-World Scenario
authors: Muyuan Shen, Sumit Roy, Hao Yin
abstract: This study evaluates the performance of Wi-Fi-based indoor localization utilizing Fine Timing Measurement (FTM) as specified in the IEEE 802.11az standard. Under line-of-sight (LOS) conditions with additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN), we quantitatively analyze the influence of key parameters on localization accuracy, including geometric dilution of precision (GDOP), the number of measurements per responding station (RSTA), receiver signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), and channel bandwidth. These effects are validated through simulations conducted in a three-dimensional office environment using the open-source ns-3 network simulator. Additionally, we investigate the impact of measurement time budgets and background data traffic on localization accuracy, as well as the effects of high-priority FTM sessions on network throughput.

title: Research on the GDP Growth Effect of Domestic Demand Expansion from the Perspective of Dual Circulation
authors: Ying Wang, Yadong Ning
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Information Economy, Data Modeling and Cloud Computing
abstract: None

title: Research on the impact of digital economy on high-quality economic development in provinces along the Silk Road Economic Belt - A Combined Econometric and Machine Learning Approach
authors: Yongjie Gui, Lixing Zhu, Xue Yang, Xiong Deng, Zhenfeng Zhu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Information Economy, Data Modeling and Cloud Computing
abstract: None

title: Induced Voltage-Based Optimization of Grounding Intervals for MV Busbar Enclosures
authors: H. Akdemir, A.C. Yalcin, B. Dursun, C. C. Uydur
abstract: Methods for establishing Medium-voltage (MV) grounding systems, which can be dangerous and costly, are typically based on traditional practices or on unscientific assumptions (e.g., fixed 10–20 meter intervals). Consequently, these methods fail to consider the geometry of conductors and high load currents which can lead to unsafe touch voltages and excessive installation costs. This research establishes a scientifically based method, grounded in the laws of physics and compliant with IEC 60364, for determining grounding connection intervals, and determines the electromagnetic coupling between phase conductors and metal enclosures using measured mutual inductance data and COMSOL Multiphysics simulations. This approach of has enabled the determination of the induced voltage gradients associated with MV busbar connection systems across various current levels (up to 5000 amps) and voltage classes (12-24 kV). Therefore, this research has provided an understanding of the physical characteristics that govern grounding system connections. The results indicate that the maximum safe grounding distances (Lmax), found to range from 120.3 m for 950 A systems to 33.5 m for high-capacity 5000 A systems, depend strongly on system design parameters. The sensitivity analysis indicates that Lmax is most influenced by fluctuations in load current and geometric clearances, and therefore provides practical guidance for incorporating strategic safety multipliers (0.8 and 0.6) into engineering applications. Additionally, this study provides a scalable framework to ensure that all grounding system designs meet the 50 V safety standard while minimizing over-engineering. In addition, this study enables the application of experimental or experience-based knowledge to the design of modern electrical infrastructure, going beyond simple estimates and providing a scientific framework for grounding system designs.

title: Cooperative Network Learning for Large-Scale and Decentralized Graphs
authors: Qiang Wu, Yiming Huang, Yujie Zeng, Yijie Teng, Fang Zhou, Linyuan Lü
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Graph representation learning has become a vital tool for capturing intricate relationships within networked systems. However, as graphs scale up, data privacy concerns and regulatory restrictions often isolate data within different institutions, hindering collaborative analysis. While federated learning (FL) and swarm learning (SL) offer decentralized solutions, FL relies on a central coordinator, and SL struggles with non-I.I.D. graph data, limiting their flexibility and applicability to complex graph structures. Therefore, establishing a mutual trust mechanism among graph agencies is crucial to unlocking the full potential of graphs. In this article, we introduce a cooperative network learning (CNL) framework to ensure secure graph computing across diverse graph tasks. CNL unifies local and global graph neural network (GNN) perspectives through secure virtual graph connections, enabling collaborative training on decentralized graphs without centralized coordination, while homomorphic encryption and secure communication further preserve privacy. Moreover, CNL enables each agency to maintain autonomy by designing heterogeneous models tailored to its own tasks, going beyond FL and SL. Extensive experiments on contagion dynamics prediction and various traditional graph tasks demonstrate that our CNL architecture outperforms state-of-the-art GNNs developed at individual sites, revealing that CNL provides a reliable, fair, secure, privacy-preserving, and global perspective for effective and personalized network applications. This framework promises to address privacy concerns in graph-related research and integrate decentralized graph data structures to benefit the network research community in cooperation and innovation.

title: Frequency-Dependent TDDB Modeling Based on Trapping Dynamics
authors: X. Federspiel, X. Garros, A. Viey, M. Arabi, J. Hai, F. Cacho, C. Diouf, D. Roy
abstract: In this article, we present a new modeling approach to analyze the effect of pulsed stress frequency on the time to breakdown of high-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math> </inline-formula> metal gates in CMOS transistor devices. First, we compare DC time-dependent dielectric breakdown (TDDB) and AC TDDB results obtained from samples fabricated using 28 nm FDSOI technology from STMicroelectronics. Then, we review the possible hypotheses that could explain the improvement in gate dielectric time to breakdown under pulsed stress. We provide details of a trapping-based TDDB model and compare the modeled effects of stress frequency on key TDDB parameters, such as median time to breakdown and voltage acceleration exponent, with experimental results. Finally, we apply the model findings to digital and RF circuit test cases to investigate the impact of frequency effects on product reliability margins.

title: Progress and Perils of PINNs: Perspectives on Applying Physics-Informed Neural Networks to Science and Engineering Problems
authors: David Hyde, Chennakesava Kadapa, Abdallah Alalem Albustami, Ahmad F. Taha
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) encode physical models such as partial or ordinary differential equations into the loss functions of neural networks. Over the past several years, PINNs have seen applications in solid mechanics, computational fluid dynamics, material modeling, and dynamics and control, covering a range of disciplines in science and engineering. However, PINNs, at least in their naïve form, generally struggle to converge to ground truth solutions, fail to strictly enforce known physical laws, and suffer from various other numerical issues (including those inherited from learning pipelines and algorithms). Thus, we have found the community to be divided over the application of PINNs to real-world science and engineering problems. This article addresses this controversy by briefly reviewing the promise of PINNs, by examining various failure modes of PINN-based pipelines, by deriving and applying PINNs for power system simulation as a case study, and finally, by suggesting future directions for research in this area.

title: Joint Resource and Trajectory Optimization for UAV-Assisted Emergency Semantic Communication
authors: Minna Huang, Kun Zhu, Yuanyuan Xu
abstract: The high flexibility and mobility enable unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) playing a pivotal role in gathering/relaying data in emergency communication. However, communication resources are scarce in emergency communication scenarios, while Semantic Communication (SC) holds promise for overcoming current communication bottlenecks by reducing data transmission volumes. In this paper, we investigate joint resource optimization and trajectory optimization in UAV-assisted emergency semantic communication networks to further enhance communication efficiency. Firstly, considering the importance of data freshness in emergency communication scenarios, we propose a semantic metric, termed semantic transmission efficiency, which integrates both semantic similarity and semantic Age of Information (AoI). Then, we jointly optimize user bandwidth, transmission power, and UAV flight trajectories with the objective of maximizing the long-term semantic transmission efficiency. To solve the joint optimization problem, we propose the Diffusion-Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (Diffusion-DDPG) algorithm. By leveraging the capability of generative diffusion model to capture data distributions, the algorithm effectively identifies optimal solutions to complex optimization problems, thereby enhancing the exploration ability of agent. Comparative experiments demonstrate that Diffusion-DDPG effectively balances semantic similarity and semantic AoI within semantic transmission efficiency. Particularly in resource-constrained scenarios, it shows superior convergence speeds.

title: Attention-Driven Deep Reinforcement Learning for Energy-Efficient Trajectory and Beamforming Design in UAV-RIS Systems
authors: Zhuo Fan, Lin Zhang, Tao Zhang, Yafeng Wang, Sheng Wu, Zhiyu Han
abstract: UAV-mounted Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are emerging as a transformative technology for energy-efficient Internet of Things (IoT) communications. However, jointly optimizing the UAV’s 3D trajectory and the RIS’s passive beamforming to serve multiple distributed IoT devices presents a challenging non-convex problem. Existing Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) approaches, such as DDPG and TD3, typically rely on monolithic Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) to process device states. This architecture suffers from a fundamental "representation bottleneck": it requires concatenating device states into a fixed-length vector, which obscures the geometric topology and fails to capture the permutation-invariant nature of multi-device systems. To address this, we propose an Attention- Augmented TD3 (A-TD3) algorithm. Unlike standard baselines, A-TD3 incorporates a self-attention mechanism to explicitly model the relational dependencies among IoT devices, enabling the agent to dynamically prioritize critical IoT devices based on their real-time channel conditions and QoS requirements. We formulate the problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) aimed at maximizing the system’s global energy efficiency. Extensive simulations demonstrate that A-TD3 outperforms standard DDPG and TD3 by over 60% and 35% in energy efficiency, respectively, particularly in dense IoT scenarios, validating its superior scalability and robustness.

title: Robust Sensor-Based Activity Recognition via Physics-Aware Disentangled Representation Learning and Feature-wise Linear Modulation Conditioning
authors: Gyuyeon Lim, Myung-Kyu Yi
abstract: Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition (HAR) often breaks down in real-world deployments because models entangle intrinsic activity patterns with extrinsic nuisance factors such as sensor orientation changes and placement shifts. This work argues that reliable HAR requires explicitly separating user motion from sensor-induced artifacts. We propose a physics-aware disentanglement framework that treats such nuisance factors as interference to be suppressed rather than patterns to be modeled. First, we inject physical priors, including gravity direction and motion smoothness, via a Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) mechanism that conditions the learned latent representations with respect to physically meaningful characteristics. Second, we adopt a dual-branch architecture in which activity-relevant and nuisance-related representations are explicitly separated by minimizing the Hilbert–Schmidt Independence Criterion (HSIC). Experiments on four public wearable HAR benchmarks show that the proposed method substantially improves robustness to sensor rotation, signal distortions, and other perturbations, while preserving high recognition accuracy and computational efficiency compared with baseline models.

title: MA-enhanced Mixed Near-field and Far-field Covert Communications
authors: Chao Zhou, Changsheng You, Cong Zhou, Hai Lin, Yi Gong
abstract: In this paper, we propose to employ a modular-based movable extremely large-scale array (XL-array) at Alice for enhancing covert communication performance. Compared with existing work that mostly considered either far-field or near-field covert communications, we consider in this paper a more general and practical mixed-field scenario, where multiple Bobs are located in either the near-field or far-field of Alice, in the presence of multiple near-field Willies. Specifically, we first consider a two-Bob-one-Willie system and show that conventional fixed-position XL-arrays suffer degraded sum-rate performance due to the energy-spread effect in mixed-field systems, which, however, can be greatly improved by subarray movement. On the other hand, for transmission covertness, it is revealed that sufficient angle difference between far-field Bob and Willie as well as adequate range difference between near-field Bob and Willie are necessary for ensuring covertness in fixed-position XL-array systems, while this requirement can be relaxed in movable XL-array systems thanks to flexible channel correlation control between Bobs and Willie. Next, for general system setups, we formulate an optimization problem to maximize the achievable weighted sum-rate under covertness constraint. To solve this non-convex optimization problem, we first decompose it into two subproblems, corresponding to an inner problem for beamforming optimization given positions of subarrays and an outer problem for subarray movement optimization. Although these two subproblems are still non-convex, we obtain their high-quality solutions by using the successive convex approximation technique and devising a customized differential evolution algorithm, respectively. Last, numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of proposed movable XL-array in balancing sum-rate and covert communication requirements, as compared to various benchmark schemes.

title: Part-level Semantic Fusion for Sketch-based 3D Voxel Reconstruction
authors: Fei Wang, Yanlong Pan, Junkun Jiang, Dazhi Jiang, Baoquan Zhao, Xiaonan Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Reconstructing 3D shapes from monocular freehand sketches is challenging due to their fragmented structures, varying line thickness, and discontinuity. These characteristics cause ambiguity, making it difficult for existing methods to extract sufficient geometric feature information to distinguish subtle shape variations and internal details of the object contours depicted in the sketches, resulting in poor overall reconstruction quality. To address these challenges, we introduce the Part-level Semantic Fusion (PSFusion) module, which combines local units of image features with global feature guidance to enhance the representation of complex geometric structures and subtle contour variations. This approach reduces ambiguity in complex edges and local details, improving shape preservation and edge contour accuracy. Additionally, we propose the coarse-to-fine 3D Decoder consisting of one 3D convolutional network as a coarse-grained regressor and one Shuffle-UNet-based fine-grained refiner, to capture feature dependencies across spatial and channel dimensions. Shuffle operations facilitate information exchange among sub-features, enhancing structural differentiation and cross-feature dependency modelling. This significantly improves the handling of subtle textures and complex intersection boundaries. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks show that our method outperforms baseline approaches, as demonstrated by both quantitative and qualitative results.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Transfer Learning Based on N-BEATS in Forecasting Univariate Time Series
authors: Minwoo Lee, Youngmi Lee, Gwangsu Kim
abstract: Transfer learning has emerged as a powerful technique in machine learning, wherein knowledge acquired from one task or dataset is leveraged to improve performance on related tasks and datasets. Although univariate time series forecasting plays a pivotal role in numerous real-world applications, the adoption of transfer learning in this domain has been relatively underexplored. This paper proposes a new transfer learning approach for univariate time series forecasting, combining the Neural Basis Expansion Analysis for interpretable Time Series forecasting (N-BEATS) and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Furthermore, the Boltzmann-weighted ensemble is proposed for prediction, serving as an advanced inference method that improves forecasting accuracy without incurring significant computational overhead. We constructed a pre-trained model for knowledge transfer. Then, we fine-tuned it to predict time series data from seven domains (temperature, solar radiation, electricity demand, air pollution, wind speed, traffic volume, and coin price). Our experimental results show that transfer learning enhances predictive performance, and investigations into additional layers reveal that MLP architectures are superior to long short-term memory and original transformer architectures in terms of Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), whereas the advanced time-series transformer PatchTST shows potential in transfer learning. Additionally, we conducted various experiments for qualitative analyses. The results of experiments consistently demonstrate the superior performance of transfer learning for univariate time series forecasting, offering valuable insights into designing more robust deep learning solutions in data-driven environments.

title: Reduced-Complexity Guessing Codeword Decoding of BCH Codes with Most Reliable Cyclic Basis
authors: Yiwen Wang, Qianfan Wang, Xiangping Zheng, Linqi Song, Xiao Ma
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced guessing codeword decoding (GCD) algorithm, termed GCD with most reliable cyclic basis (MRCB-GCD), specifically tailored for BCH codes. Unlike original GCD, the proposed method selects the k consecutive bits with the highest aggregate reliability as the re-encoding basis, leveraging the cyclic structure of BCH codes. To further enhance search efficiency, a local constraint (LC) mechanism is introduced, where the extended reliable bits of length k+δ enable the algorithm to skip numerous unnecessary test error patterns (TEPs). To stop the search process, we introduce two termination criteria and prove that the proposed approach with the trivial termination criterion forms a non-exhaustive-search maximum likelihood (ML) decoding algorithm. A saddlepoint-based numerical method is developed to approximately calculate the upper bound on the performance gap to the ML decoding and estimate the average number of searches, showing the advantages of the proposed MRCB-GCD over the original GCD in terms of both decoding performance and search efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that: 1) MRCB- GCD outperforms original GCD in both frame error rate (FER) and number of searches, 2) the LC mechanism and termination criteria further reduce the number of searches, and 3) the proposed approach achieves finite-length capacity across various code rates but without requiring Gaussian elimination.

title: Near-Field Variable-Width Beam Coverage and Codebook Design for XL-RIS
authors: Yida Zhang, Qiuyan Liu, Qiang Wang, Hongtao Luo, Yuqi Xia
abstract: To mitigate the issue of limited base station coverage caused by severe high-frequency electromagnetic wave attenuation, Extremely Large Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (XL-RIS) has garnered significant attention due to its high beam gain. However, XL-RIS exhibits a narrower beam width compared to traditional RIS, which increases the complexity of beam alignment and broadcast. To address this problem, we propose a variable-width beam generation algorithm under the near-field assumption and apply it to the near-field codebook design for XL-RIS. Our algorithm can achieve beam coverage for arbitrarily shaped codeword regions and generate a joint codebook for the multi-XL-RIS system. The simulation results demonstrate that our proposed scheme enables user equipment (UE) to achieve higher spectral efficiency and lower communication outage probability within the codeword region compared to existing works. Furthermore, our scheme exhibits better robustness to codeword region location and area variations.

title: Deep Learning-Based Radar Sensing for Off-the-Shelf Communication Transceivers
authors: Kaikai Liu
abstract: Recent research has shown a growing trend toward leveraging existing communication systems for radar sensing capabilities, creating dual-function systems that optimize hardware resources. This paper presents our prototype system using Analog Devices’ software-defined radio transceivers for communication with an additional frequency sweeping modulator for radar, enabling communication systems to perform radar functions. Our approach incorporates simulation setups for traditional FMCW radar as well as our proposed OFDM radar, and hybrid OFDM-FMCW and Sine-FMCW configurations that closely mirror our hardware system’s two-step process. This enhances OFDM communication systems with radar sensing capabilities, facilitating straightforward transfer to real-world implementations. We also introduce a novel approach to radar target detection using deep learning techniques that can process both time-domain and frequency-domain radar data. Our flexible neural network architecture can learn directly from raw time-domain signals or from pre-processed range-Doppler maps. Simulation results demonstrate that our hybrid communication and radar system achieves performance similar to traditional FMCW radar with high detection accuracy while remaining robust to noise and interference. We have also transferred the trained model from simulation data to our prototype system, showing promising range-Doppler detection results.

title: Hierarchical Multimodal Motion Control of Magnetic Pivot-Walking Millirobotic-Grippers for Autonomous Target Acquisition in Complex Terrains_supp4-3675526.mp4
authors: Huaping Wang
abstract: Magnetic soft millirobotic-grippers, equipped with agile pivot-walking motions and adaptive enveloping morph abilities, hold great promise for biomedical target acquisition tasks. However, deploying these millirobotic-grippers in highly constrained, disturbed, and variable terrains to realize multisequence target acquisition and transport tasks remains challenging. Here, we introduce a hierarchical multimodal motion control method for pivot-walking magnetic milliroboticgrippers, which enhances adaptive locomotion capabilities and enables high-precision motion control, facilitating autonomous target acquisition in complex terrains. The millirobotic-gripper utilizes a centrosymmetric three-pivot design, enabling adaptive soft enveloping deformation and robust multimodal locomotion. A hierarchical control architecture is proposed, comprising: 1) an upper-level Event-Based Finite State Machine planner that dynamically orchestrates transitions between motion modes according to environmental feedback and task-specific conditions; and 2) a lower-level Sliding Mode controller integrated with Gaussian Process-based gait parameter optimization, significantly improving motion accuracy and robustness against environmental disturbances. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method allows millirobotic-grippers to efficiently navigate morphing tunnels, leap across gaps exceeding 3 times their body length, accurately follow arbitrary paths with errors less than 5% of their body length, and reliably perform three types of targets grasping and transport. Furthermore, the biomedical application potential of our system is initially validated through ex vivo porcine gastrointestinal tract experiment with ultrasound guidance.

title: Closed-Loop Self-Assembly and Navigation of Magnetic Modular Millibots in Confined Environments_supp4-3675474.mp4
authors: MINJUN KIM
abstract: Magnetic modular millibots, capable of deterministic self-assembly and reconfiguration under wireless magnetic fields, offer a promising route toward mesoscale manipulation in structured and confined environments. This work presents a modular chain millibot composed of cubic units with free-to-spin internal magnets that align under external fields. While individual cubes cannot propel independently, their assembly into chains enables controlled locomotion through sliding and tumbling modes. We first demonstrate open-loop operation in confined workspaces, including chain formation, navigation, controlled disassembly, and wall climbing, supported by a dynamic model and parametric analysis that identify the actuation and geometric conditions required for successful climbing. Building on this foundation, we introduce a closed-loop control framework that integrates vision-based feedback. As the millibot’s movement speed increases with chain length, the order of assembly strongly affects task completion time; we therefore formulate the sequence-planning problem as a harmonic traveling salesman problem (HTSP) and solve it to compute deterministic cube-collection sequences that minimize the effective travel cost. The controller applies sliding and tumbling dynamics to realize obstacle-aware navigation and reliable self-assembly. Experiments validate autonomous assembly of four cubes with a two-cube chain into a six-cube chain in free space and collection of three cubes with wall climbing in a confined workspace, both with 100% success. The measured mean unit travel times were 0.67 s/mm in free space and 0.89 s/mm in confined environments. Collectively, these results establish a robust automation framework for reversible mesoscale self-assembly, programmable navigation, and lab-on-chip applications.

title:
Bi
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    CFEL: Continual Feature Evolution Learning for Industrial Online Recognition
authors: Keke Huang, Weiyi Feng, Zixuan Chen, Dehao Wu, Chunhua Yang, Weihua Gui
abstract: Image data serve as a vital information carrier in industrial systems, but closed-set recognition methods falter in dynamic online environments with continually emerging classes. Online learning adapts rapidly but suffers from catastrophic forgetting, while continual learning alleviates forgetting through regularization yet depends on offline training, limiting adaptability to rapid task shifts. Resource-constrained edge devices demand real-time model updates, where streaming few-shot novel classes introduce feature bias, reducing class separability and accelerating model degradation. To this end, this work proposes the Biscale Storage and bilevel contrast-based continual feature evolution learning (Bi<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CFEL) for industrial online recognition. By integrating biscale storage and bilevel contrast, Bi<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CFEL efficiently memorizes and captures intraclass and interclass information, enabling accurate and robust image recognition in industrial online scenarios. First, spatio-temporal biscale guided storage strategy is innovatively proposed, constructing a representative and discriminative historical storage through class prototype distance screening criterion and prototype calibration mechanism. Second, to address overfitting risks in limited storage, distribution-aware memory reactivation method reactivates old-class memory based on stored sample distributions, effectively suppressing feature drift and forgetting. Finally, bilevel contrastive dictionary learning framework optimizes high-dimensional sparse feature spaces via instance-prototype cross-level contrast mechanisms, enhancing intraclass cohesion and interclass separation to decouple old and new class features. Experimental results demonstrate that Bi<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CFEL achieves state-of-the-art performance in generalization capability, forgetting rate, and overall process performance.

title: RIS-SCMA Co-design for Endogenous Security and Spectral Efficiency: A Multi-Agent DRL Approach in Cognitive Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Zhi Lin, Haotong Cao, Zimo Feng, Tamer Mohamed Abdellatif, Sherif Moussa
abstract: To address the critical security challenges posed by the inherent broadcasting nature and heterogeneous service demands in cognitive satellite-terrestrial networks (CSTN), this paper presents a groundbreaking framework that integrates reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) with sparse code multiple access (SCMA). This framework aims to maximize the achievable secrecy rate by jointly optimizing transmit beamforming, the RIS reflection matrix, and SCMA codebook configurations, while adhering to power constraints and the quality-of-service requirements of legitimate users. To tackle the non-convex optimization problem in complex environments, we develop an intelligent decision-making mechanism based on a modified multi-agent two-delay deep deterministic (MMTD3) algorithm, which introduces a breakthrough mechanism by decoupling continuous beam control from discrete codebook selection, offering a new paradigm for AI-driven cross-domain security optimization in CSTN. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms existing benchmarks, verifying its potential in supporting wireless endogenous security and meeting massive heterogeneous service demands in CSTN.

title: Throughput Analysis of Coded Slotted ALOHA with Imperfect Interference Cancellation
authors: Pin-Ting Wang, Duan-Shin Lee, Cheng-Shang Chang
abstract: In this paper we consider coded slotted ALOHA (CSA) systems, in which each packet arrival is duplicated and transmitted to receivers randomly. Successfully received packets are used to cancel their interference with the reception of other packets. In this paper, we assume that interference cancellation is imperfect and that mean channel estimation errors follow an inverse square root law. We approximate the probability of correct reception of a message by the probability that the signal-to-interference-and-noise ratio (SINR) of the message exceeds a threshold. We derive a conditional probability of successful reception of a packet for a location model of transmitters and receivers, given the number of additional packet arrivals and the number of packets successfully received in previous iterations. We estimate this conditional probability by a mean field approximation. Under these assumptions, we perform an and-or tree analysis. We verify through extensive simulations that the mean field approximation and the and-or tree analysis work quite well. Simulation results show that throughput improvement of coded slotted Aloha systems with imperfect interference cancellation can be significantly lower that that of a system with perfect cancellation.

title: VerTouch: A Versatile Training System for Hand Function by Exploring Tactile Composite Perception
authors: Xiaotong Shi, Junhan Zhang, Shaojie Chen, Yixuan Liu, Shibo Han
abstract: Hand function impairment induced by factors like stroke exerts a serious impact on human life. Hand function training systems, as key technologies for enhancing hand operation ability, have attracted extensive attention. However, existing systems commonly provide only a single-dimensional function, but overlook the composite characteristics of human tactile perception, resulting in poor practicality. To this end, we present VerTouch, a versatile training system for hand function by exploring tactile perception signatures. Specifically, we first investigate the core algorithms utilized in system implementation, including: kinesthetic function quantitative assessment, low-cost haptic signal reconstruction, and haptic perception test threshold generation. Subsequently, the operational realization of VerTouch is described. The system’s hardware framework encompasses a kinesthetic force-feedback interaction module and a haptic recognition module, augmented by a visual software interface. Finally, the key technologies in the VerTouch system are extensive tested, and the experimental results demonstrate that this system has the capacity to satisfy the requirements of compound hand function training.

title: A Random Forest- Powered Defense Against Malicious URLs
authors: Mukesh Kumar Tripathi, M. Neelakantappa, Ganesh Deshmukh, Chirumani Pradyumna Reddy, Charaka Ananya, Msykg Franzonia
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: In these current digital systems the website based cyberattacks has become a main problem for government and private companies. Reports show that there are more than 1.5 lakh Indian websites which we are been targeted from 2014 to 2025 showing the importance of the issue. Since website related applications are now important for everyday activities, protecting them has become a big task and so important. Unfortunately, the regular detecting methods always fail to meet these requirements. Many other models which exist show false accuracyy such that it may cause always false alarms, and need significant computing power importantly when working with very big datasets. To overcome these limits, this study shows a machine learning dependent approach which is made up using the Random Forest algorithm. Random Forest gives the user more constant and very well performance when compared to traditional methods which include models as SVM, Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, and Boost, which always struggle stay up to date with advancing attack related patterns. It results in more accuracy, lessens the false positives, and delivers good output making it good suited for noticing the challenges of today's cybersecurity environment. This also highlights that the attackers usually use malicious URLs to trick users and steal important information. As their methods become more advanced older detection systems fail to provide good defiance. Our study shows that Random Forest can fill this gap by offering a more robust, accurate, and cost-effective solution for protecting (securing) web applications.

title: Non-Contact Multi-Dimensional Electrical-Thermal-Acoustic Condition Sensing for Converter Stations
authors: Wentong Shang, Jiayun Zhu, Yingke Mao, Yi Ding
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Converter valves operate within the confined spaces of converter stations, which restricts the spatial arrangement of condition monitoring equipment. High-frequency electromagnetic interference generated by the switching of valve modules adversely affects the normal operation of the converter valve's condition detection equipment. Currently, there is a lack of research on noncontact multi-dimensional electrical-thermal-acoustic condition sensing and evaluation specifically for converter valves. This paper proposes non-contact sensing technologies for weak discharge, long-distance temperature, and wide-spectrum acoustic waves in converter valves. It develops a multi-dimensional electrical, thermal, and acoustic sensing device based on: Silicon Photomultiplier (SiPM) for discharge sensing, infrared thermography for component-level temperature detection of valve equipment, and microphone arrays for non-contact acoustic wave sensing. This provides a foundation for supporting the engineering application and reliable operation of new converter valve technologies.

title: Small Target Detection in UAV Remote Sensing Images Based on Adaptive Cross-modal Feature Fusion and Attention Mechanism
authors: Zhe Yuan, Baolong Guo, Jiawei Song, Junbin Zhuang, Yichuan Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Small target detection in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) remote sensing images faces challenges such as insufficient feature information and background interference in complex environments. Existing single-modal methods, which rely solely on one data source, struggle to address these issues effectively. This paper proposes CA-YOLO, a lightweight detection framework based on adaptive cross-modal feature fusion and an attention mechanism. The framework employs a dual-branch backbone network to extract complementary features from RGB and IR modalities, utilizes an Adaptive Cross-modal Feature Fusion (ACFF) module to dynamically integrate global semantics and local details, and leverages a Lightweight Hybrid Attention (LHA) mechanism to enhance targets and suppress noise. Experiments on three public datasets, DroneVehicle, LLVIP, and FLIR, demonstrate that CA-YOLO achieves mAPs of 75.1%, 96.1%, and 71.4%, respectively, while requiring only 6.8 MB parameters and 16.4 GFLOPs computational cost, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods. The inference speeds reach 57.1 FPS, 78.1 FPS, and 56.5 FPS on the respective datasets, achieving an optimal balance between accuracy and efficiency. Ablation studies and visualization results further validate the effectiveness of each module and the framework's superior capability in reducing missed detection and false alarm rates.

title: CNC Machine Tool Thermal Error Prediction Model Optimized by Genetic Algorithm-Based Bi-GRU
authors: Yongdong Qu, Pengtao Nan, Jin Deng, Yu Ye
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Intelligent Manufacturing, and Automation Technology (MEMAT)
abstract: Aiming at the problems that the thermal error of CNC machine tools is affected by multi-source temperature coupling, has complex temporal characteristics, and the hyperparameters of traditional models rely on empirical tuning, a thermal error prediction model based on Genetic Algorithm (GA)optimized Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (Bi-GRU) is proposed. Firstly, the temperature difference sensitive point data and spindle X-direction thermal error data under different working conditions of the machine tool are collected, and a timeseries dataset is constructed through standardization preprocessing. Secondly, a network architecture containing two bidirectional GRU layers is designed to capture the bidirectional temporal dependence between temperature changes and thermal errors. Then, the genetic algorithm is adopted to globally optimize the key hyperparameters of the Bi-GRU model, including the number of GRU units, dropout rate, learning rate, and batch size, which solves the defects of low efficiency and easy trapping in local optima of traditional tuning methods. Finally, the model performance is verified through comparative experiments. The results show that the GA-optimized Bi-GRU model achieves a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.032 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.021 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> on the test set. Compared with the unoptimized Bi-GRU model, the prediction accuracy is improved by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8. 4 \%}, \mathbf{2 5. 7 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 1. 2 \%}$</tex> respectively. Moreover, the model has stronger generalization ability and can accurately capture the dynamic evolution law of thermal errors of CNC machine tools, providing a reliable basis for machine tool accuracy compensation.

title: Deep Enhanced Control of Hybrid Switches for Transformers in Flexible Transmission and Distribution Systems Based on an Improved NSGA-III Algorithm
authors: Jiarong Lin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: In transformer hybrid switch control, only voltage fluctuation is considered, resulting in a relatively concentrated distribution of reference points, which cannot adapt to transformer operating requirements. This leads to inconsistent control results with actual conditions, compromising transformer safety. Therefore, a deep-enhanced control method for hybrid switches in flexible power transmission and distribution systems is designed based on an improved NSGA-III algorithm. Based on the hybrid switch operation sequence, the current mutation and voltage fluctuation at the time of control action are calculated, and a control model for hybrid switches in flexible power transmission and distribution systems is constructed. The distribution of reference points in the hybrid switch control model is analyzed, and the NSGA-III algorithm is improved by setting an adaptive population size. Two reference point sets are obtained, and the optimal gain of the transformer hybrid switch control model is generated, thus achieving deep-enhanced control of the transformer hybrid switch. The final control results show that after deep-enhanced control of the hybrid switch, the load voltage fluctuates around 900 V, while the transformer voltage remains stable. The hybrid switch control effect is excellent, playing an important role in the stable operation of transformers in flexible power transmission and distribution systems.

title: Enhanced Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using PSO-Tuned Deep Belief Networks: A Hybrid Learning Approach
authors: Rajitha Ala, Leema Nelson, Mukta Jagdish
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: This Early and accurate detection of breast cancer is crucial for better treatment results and patient survival rates. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning method that combines Deep Belief Networks (DBNs) with Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to increase diagnostic accuracy. The proposed model uses the hierarchical feature extraction capabilities of DBNs and the global optimization power of PSO to modify hyper parameters such as learning rate and hidden layer size. The widely used Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (WBCD) is used to evaluate the model's performance. Experimental results show that the PSO-optimized DBN model achieves a test accuracy of 97%, outperforming traditional classifiers such as Naive Bayes, Decision Tree, and K-Nearest Neighbors. Additional measures that validate the model include precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. Other metrics, including as precision, recall, and F1-score, further validate the model's resilience. The hybrid PSO-DBN framework's strong generalization, little overfitting, and outstanding dependability make it a promising tool for computer-aided diagnosis and intelligent breast cancer screening.

title: Holographic Active RIS-aided for Robust Secure Uplink Near-Field NOMA Networks
authors: Sandeep Singh, Keshav Singh, Sandeep Kumar Singh, Fan-Shuo Tseng, Aryan Kaushik
abstract: This article proposes a holographic active reconfigurable intelligent surface (HARIS)-aided near-field (NF)-driven uplink non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) secure communication system under imperfect channel state information (iCSI) in the presence of an eavesdropper (Eve). To ensure efficient resource utilization, a sum secrecy rate (SSR) maximization problem is formulated by jointly optimizing the combined vector at the base station (BS), the power allocation for each uplink user, and the HARIS phase profile under strict quality of service (QoS) requirements and limited power budgets at both the uplink users and HARIS. Due to the non-convex nature of the problem, an alternating optimization (AO)-based algorithm is proposed, which uses semidefinite programming (SDP), convex upper bound approximation, and semidefinite relaxation (SDR) techniques to optimize all variables iteratively. Extensive simulations validate the performance and convergence of the proposed algorithm. In addition, the effects of system parameters, such as the number of HARIS elements, minimum QoS per user, maximum BS receive power, and the HARIS amplification factor, are investigated.

title: Robotic In-Hand Sensorless Texture Discrimination through Rubbing Motion and the Stick-Slip Phenomenon
authors: Juan Padron, Yusuke Kawai, Kiyoshi Ohishi, Kouhei Ohnishi
publish: 2026 IEEE 19th International Conference on Advanced Motion Control (AMC)
abstract: Humans can easily infer surface roughness by lightly rubbing an object with a fingertip, but achieving similar in-hand texture perception remains challenging for robotic hands—especially without fingertip sensors. This study investigates what discriminative features can be extracted from simple rubbing motions performed by a robotic hand when only actuator current references and joint velocity/position measurements are available. Experiments with three materials show that the stick–slip phenomenon emerging during rubbing provides measurable cues related to surface texture. In particular, the harmonic content and total harmonic distortion (THD) derived from finger velocity and estimated disturbance frequency response differ consistently between smooth and rough materials. Based on these findings, we propose three features that enable discrimination between smooth and rough textures without using fingertip sensors.

title: Multi-Target Position Error Bound and Power Allocation Scheme for Cell-Free mMIMO-OTFS ISAC Systems
authors: Yifei Fan, Shaochuan Wu, Haojie Wang, Mingjun Sun, Jianhe Wang
abstract: This paper investigates multi-target position estimation in cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF mMIMO) architectures, where orthogonal time frequency and space (OTFS) is used as an integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) signal. Closed-form expressions for the Cramér-Rao lower bound and the positioning error bound (PEB) in multi-target position estimation are derived, providing quantitative evaluations of sensing performance. To enhance the overall performance of the ISAC system, a power allocation algorithm is developed to maximize the minimum user communication signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio while ensuring a specified sensing PEB requirement. The results validate the proposed PEB expression and its approximation, clearly illustrating the coordination gain enabled by ISAC. Further, the superiority of using the multi-static CF mMIMO architecture over traditional cellular ISAC is demonstrated, and the advantages of OTFS signals in high-mobility scenarios are highlighted.

title: Role of Social Media in Enhancing Employee Resilience: Moderating Influence of Individual Adaptability
authors: Dhanya Krishnan, Saravanan V, Vidhya S, Pandian P
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: In fast-paced corporate world, it's more important than ever to be powerful. Being strong involves being able to bounce back quickly after a setback and be calm when things get rough. The fact that so many people use social media has also changed how individuals deal with stress, do their jobs, and talk to each other. Researchers have investigated how social media affects people's mental health at work a lot less than they have investigated ways to make individuals stronger. Even if social media is becoming a major part of the company's culture, there is still no strong evidence that using these sites makes employees more resilient. Also, research in the actual world nearly never looks at how different aspects affect this connection. This study uses a quantitative survey-based method. 512 workers from different businesses in Tamil Nadu, India, took part in the survey. We used scales that have been evaluated to find out how resilient, adaptable, and active people are on social media. We used AMOS 26.0 to do Structural Equation Modelling (SEM) to look at both direct and moderated interactions. We used Hayes' PROCESS macro (Model 1) in SPSS 27.0 to do a moderation study that looked at the connection between employees' use of social media and their capacity to get back on their feet after failures. The results reveal that employees who use social media are more resilient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.42, p<0.01)$</tex>. People who are more adaptable are more likely to be strong when they utilize social media a lot. A person's level of flexibility can affect this connection; a higher level of adaptation helps to explain the link. The model fit indicators, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CFI}=0.94$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{RMSEA}=0.05$</tex>, proved that the model was correct. Researchers found that this model's explained variance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R 2}$</tex>) was 13 to 19 percent higher than the variances of the stress-management predictors that were already in place.

title: Ensemble Learning Paradigm for Stock Market Prediction
authors: Nausheen Ansari, Tejoram Vakada, Anisha Mahato, Mansi Rawat, Daya Satya Sagar Vakada
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Stock market prediction plays a vital role in modern finance, helping investors forecast asset values with greater confidence. This research applies machine learning, focusing on stacking, which integrates models like Decision Trees, SVMs, and Gradient Boosting to enhance accuracy. The process involves collecting reliable financial data, followed by preprocessing and feature extraction to ensure high-quality inputs. Trained on historical data, the models capture complex patterns to generate forecasts. Stacking further refines predictions by reducing individual model biases, resulting in more reliable outcomes. Overall, this approach offers investors and analysts trustworthy insights to support data-driven decision-making.

title: Meteorological Scene-Driven PV Power Forecasting with Enhanced FCM and Downscaling
authors: Gang Zhang, Xiangyang Zhang, Ziyuan Wang, Keyue Zhu, Pingping Mei, Xinye Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of new energy sources connected to the grid, accurately predicting the output of photovoltaic power plants has become the core demand for building a new power system and optimizing the intelligent dispatching system. The prediction accuracy of traditional time series models is significantly reduced in meteorological abrupt scenes (such as sudden cloud migration and strong convective weather). In order to meet this challenge, this study proposes a dynamic prediction framework based on multi-model fusion and meteorological scene recognition. Specifically, the meteorological characteristic matrix output by NWP system is introduced, including key parameters such as solar radiation intensity, environmental temperature and humidity, atmospheric transmittance, etc., so as to construct a multivariable day-ahead prediction model with meteorological coupling ability. Based on the existing historical power forecasting framework, the model has to expand the feature space, thus establishing a nonlinear mapping relationship between meteorological factors and output fluctuation. The research needs to use comparative experimental methods to quantitatively evaluate the gain effect of meteorological data fusion on prediction accuracy, especially on early warning of extreme weather events, and to reveal the differences in sensitivity of meteorological parameters with the help of prediction error spectrum analysis of multiple meteorological scenarios, such as sunny irradiation steady state and rainy irradiation sudden metamorphosis.

title: Dual Modulation-Based Infrared-Visible Image Translation Diffusion Model for Power Line Inspection
authors: Xionggang Li, Duanjiao Li, Zhihao Zheng, Chao Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Instance segmentation in infrared images involves extracting object categories and locations, which serves as a prerequisite for determining overheating defects, and plays a crucial role in power line inspection. Compared to visible images, infrared images possess lower data interpretability, often leading to performance degradation when applying existing instance segmentation methods. Therefore, infrared to visible image translation presents an effective solution. This paper incorporates a dual modulation-based diffusion model for image translation. To reduce the object distortion problem commonly observed in existing translation methods, the proposed model proposes a semantic modulation module and a structural modulation module. The former enhances the semantic consistency of objects by aligning feature distributions between infrared and visible images, while the latter improves structural integrity through capturing rich context from infrared and visible images. These modules contribute to high-fidelity image translation, thereby overcoming the performance degradation of existing instance segmentation methods. Extensive experiment results on the power grid dataset demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method.

title: Optimal Control Method for the Dynamic Coupling Matrix of Pressure and Flow in Energy Pipeline Networks Based on Sparse Tensor Decomposition
authors: Zhihao Chen, Yexuan Jin, Jiaming Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: When optimizing the dynamic coupling matrix of pressure and flow in energy pipeline networks, optimizing only the network pressure cannot achieve ideal control results. Therefore, this paper proposes an optimal control method for the dynamic coupling matrix of pressure and flow in energy pipeline networks based on sparse tensor decomposition. Based on collected flow and pressure data from the energy pipeline network, the dynamic correlation strength between flow and pressure is calculated. Using the sparse tensor decomposition method, the constructed fourth-order sparse tensor is decomposed to extract a multi-factor matrix, and a dynamic coupling matrix model of pressure and flow in the pipeline network is constructed. Based on this, the optimization control objective function is minimized, with multiple constraints such as node flow balance and pressure threshold limits set. Using the hybrid frog leaping algorithm, the optimal control result is obtained by updating and mutating individual frogs. Experimental results show that the designed control method achieves a 55.42% reduction in flow loss, a 3.56% pressure fluctuation rate, and a 0.038 overshoot in practical applications, demonstrating good control stability.

title: Efficient Trajectory and User Assignment Design for UAV-Aided Covert Transmission against Cooperative Detection
authors: Peng Wu, Xiaopeng Yuan, Yulin Hu, Jian Zhang, Anke Schmeink
abstract: In this paper, we study efficient trajectory and user assignment design for an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-aided covert transmission against cooperative detection from multiple wardens, which is still an open issue in the literature. Starting with analysis on basic principles of cooperative detection, we derive the closed form expression of covertness metric under cooperative detection. Then a joint design of trajectory and user assignment is formulated to maximize the minimum throughput. Although the problem is highly nonconvex with infinite variables, we adopt the optimal successive-hover-and-fly (SHF) structure to reformulated the problem and reduce the complexity without loss of optimality. Then, an efficient algorithm is developed based on a convex approximation to obtain a high-quality solution. Finally, simulations verify the necessity of considering cooperative detection and the performance advantages of proposed design.

title: TeleOps: Blockchain and DL-based Optical Fiber Fault Detection Framework for Telesurgery Systems
authors: Lakshit Pathak, Mansi Thakkar, Khushi Shah, Sneha Singh, Drashti Kansara, Rajesh Gupta, Sudeep Tanwar, Joel J. P. C. Rodrigues
abstract: Telesurgery is a new medical technology wherein the surgeon is situated remotely and operates through computers on surgically interactive robotic equipment connected through high speed communications networks. Optical fibers are necessary for this process as they have low latency and high capacity for realtime control of the process and exchange of data. Nevertheless, transmission is vulnerable to disruption by optical fiber faults which is disruptive to surgery safety and accuracy. This paper introduces TeleOps framework to enhance communication over optical fibers and fault management in telesurgery systems. Deep Learning (DL) models like Feedforward Neural Network (FFNN), 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN), and Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) were implemented to detect and classify faults in Optical Time-Domain Reflectometer (OTDR) trace sequences. The RNN with Adam optimization achieved the highest detection accuracy. The proposed TeleOps framework also includes a blockchain-based smart contract to ensure transparent and encrypted tracking of faults, decentralized storage, and entity management in terms of surgical outcomes. This dual strategy provides reliable communication and efficient fault monitoring to minimize downtime of the remote surgical action. Thus the proposed TeleOps framework promises to enable safer and more effective solutions for remote healthcare by significantly enhancing the security and reliability of telesurgery systems.

title: BCI 2026 TOC
authors: N/A

title: Modeling and Economic Analysis of an Integrated Wind-PV-CSP Energy System Considering Flexible Load
authors: Xu Meng, Zhenghao Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: China's energy resources and demand exhibit an inverse spatial distribution. To address this mismatch, this study proposes a “renewable hydrogen production combined with natural gas blending” approach for cross-regional energy transfer and constructs an integrated energy system combining wind power, photovoltaic (PV), and concentrated solar power (CSP). Based on a mixed integer programming (MIP) approach, a multi-energy flow model for the CSP plant is developed, considering wind and PV output characteristics, adjustable electrolysis loads, and electricity pricing policies. This study also conducts a full-year (8760-hour) time-series simulation and economic assessment. Results indicate that the storage capability of the CSP system plays a significant role in mitigating Wind-PV fluctuations and enhancing the operational stability of water electrolysis for hydrogen production. The integrated system can reliably supply approximately 4 TWh of electricity and 3.5 million tons of steam to the park while exporting over 1.1 TWh of green electricity to the surrounding grid, maintaining strong energy supply security even under extreme weather conditions. Economic analysis shows that the project demonstrates favorable financial feasibility under both time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing schemes. The findings suggest that the Wind-PV-CSP integrated model provides an effective pathway to improve renewable energy utilization and economic performance, offering a viable technical solution and decision-making reference for cross-regional renewable energy integration and comprehensive hydrogen utilization in China.

title: Superimposed Pilot-Data Co-Design Framework with Buffer Band in OTFS System
authors: Shihao Wu, Yiran Yang, Zhaoqi Wang, Xiqing Liu, Mugen Peng
abstract: Orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation has become an indispensable key technology for high mobility communication scenarios. By accurately estimating the channel sparsity characteristics in the delay-Doppler (DD) domain, it can effectively compensate for doubly selective fading, thereby ensuring system robustness to rapidly time-varying channels. However, existing pilot designs for DD domain channel estimation cannot simultaneously meet the requirements of high estimation accuracy and low pilot overhead. To address this issue, we propose a low-power buffer band with superimposed pilot (BBSP) design scheme to jointly optimize the channel estimation performance and spectral efficiency. Low-power buffer symbols are employed to reduce the interference between pilot and data, and the adaptive Bayesian optimization algorithm is implemented for power allocation to maximize the channel capacity. Simulation results show that the BBSP scheme achieves high channel capacity with acceptable loss of estimation accuracy, demonstrating significant performance advantages over existing schemes.

title: Embedding Byzantine Fault Tolerance into Federated Learning via Consistency Scoring
authors: Youngjoon Lee, Jinu Gong, Joonhyuk Kang
abstract: Given sufficient data from multiple edge devices, federated learning (FL) enables training a shared model without transmitting private data to the central server. However, FL is generally vulnerable to Byzantine attacks from compromised edge devices, which can significantly degrade the model performance. In this work, we propose an intuitive plugin that seamlessly embeds Byzantine resilience into existing FL methods. The key idea is to generate virtual data samples and evaluate model consistency scores across local updates to effectively filter out compromised updates. By utilizing this scoring mechanism before the aggregation phase, the proposed plugin enables existing FL methods to become robust against Byzantine attacks while maintaining their original benefits. Numerical results on blood cell classification task demonstrate that the proposed plugin provides strong Byzantine resilience. In detail, plugin-attached FedAvg achieves over 89.6% test accuracy under 30% targeted attacks (vs. 19.5% w/o plugin) and maintains 65–70% test accuracy under untargeted attacks (vs. 17–19% w/o plugin).

title: Cell-Free Massive MIMO-Based Physical-Layer Authentication
authors: Isabella W. G. da Silva, Zahra Mobini, Hien Quoc Ngo, Michail Matthaiou
abstract: In this paper, we exploit the cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) architecture to design a physical-layer authentication (PLA) framework that can simultaneously authenticate multiple distributed users across the coverage area. Our proposed scheme remains effective even in the presence of active adversaries attempting impersonation attacks to disrupt the authentication process. Specifically, we introduce a tag-based PLA CF-mMIMO system, wherein the access points (APs) first estimate their channels with the legitimate users during an uplink training phase. Subsequently, a unique secret key is generated and securely shared between each user and the APs. We then formulate a hypothesis testing problem and derive a closed-form expression for the probability of detection for each user in the network. Numerical results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, demonstrating that it maintains a high detection probability even as the number of users in the system increases.

title: RNN and Bidirectional LSTM-Based Model for Fake News Detection
authors: Sumit Kumar Rana, Mahak Singh, Deepak Kumar, Shubhangi Kumari, Navdeep Singh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: During this period, the expansion of fake news boundaries has developed into a broad-spectrum matter that not only influences the way politics work but the entire society's trust in media as well. In-traditional means for making distinction between real and fake news usually failed to follow with the speedy upgrading of strategies applied to hand over misleading information. This paper describes the application of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), the most powerful yet expensive kind of deep learning network, for fake news detection and improving its accuracy and efficiency. The data used was a set with over 44,921 news articles from Counterfeit and Real News equally, for training and testing our machine. The data was processed to remove excessive data, standardize the text, and convert words into numerical features [or tokens] for use with neural networks. Our model, which can be characterized as the one based on Bidirectional Long short-term memory (LSTM) layers, has been developed as an apt description of the context of the given text, while ensuring the capability of performing the flexible processing of the text sequence. It contains the combination of the modelling dropout layers for self-stopping feature and the early stopping mechanism to get the desired performance. Using an RNN-based model, score of 99.04 % accuracy on the test set and likewise exceeded this in precision and recall metrics as well, getting scores of 99 %. The fact that these deep learning techniques marked high accuracy rates suggest that RNNs are good choice models for the identification of fake news. In addition, tabulating a confusion matrix to show the pattern of predictive capabilities in more detail. With this solution, RNN technology can be viewed as effective for revealing fake news thus serving as a way out of today's unique news related challenge.

title: Adaptive and Scalable Cluster Head Management for mMTC using Sampling-Based Agglomerative Clustering and Localized Reclustering
authors: Clément Dutriez, Omar Sami Oubbati, Cédric Gueguen, Abderrezak Rachedi
abstract: The proliferation of massive Machine-Type Communications (mMTC) in 5 G and emerging 6 G networks necessitates scalable and energy-efficient clustering solutions capable of adapting to dense and partially mobile Internet of Things (IoT) deployments. Existing Cluster Head (CH) management protocols often lack adaptability to dynamic energy conditions and node mobility, limiting their effectiveness in real-world mMTC scenarios. This paper introduces a lightweight, modular clustering framework tailored for large-scale mMTC environments. Initial cluster formation is achieved using a sampling-based agglomerative clustering technique, ensuring scalable and low-complexity cluster construction. To maintain long-term efficiency, a drift-aware reclustering mechanism detects and dismantles unstable clusters based on centroid displacement and spatial spread variation. Within each cluster, CH reelection is triggered based on residual energy, load fluctuation, or elapsed time, allowing timely and adaptive updates coordinated by a central controller. A dedicated mobility handler ensures that mobile nodes can dynamically reattach to nearby CHs, preserving cluster connectivity under frequent movement. The framework is implemented in OMNeT++ with Random Waypoint mobility and Poisson traffic models. Simulation results show significant improvements in execution time, network lifetime, packet delay, and energy efficiency compared to baseline and recent state-of-the-art protocols, demonstrating the potential of adaptive, scalable clustering for energy-efficient and resilient mMTC in next-generation IoT systems.

title: Joint Spatial Division and Multiplexing with Customized Orthogonal Group Channels in Multi-RIS-Assisted Systems
authors: Weicong Chen, Chao-Kai Wen, Wankai Tang, Xiao Li, Shi Jin
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) offer the unique capability to reshape the radio environment, thereby simplifying transmission schemes traditionally contingent on channel conditions. Joint spatial division and multiplexing (JSDM) emerges as a low-overhead transmission scheme for multi-user equipment (UE) scenarios, typically requiring complex matrix decomposition to achieve block-diagonalization of the effective channel. In this study, we introduce an innovative JSDM design that leverages RISs to customize channels, thereby streamlining the overall procedures. By strategically placing RISs along the discrete Fourier transform directions of the base station (BS), we construct orthogonal line-of-sight links within the BS-RIS channel. This approach not only simplifies the pre-beamforming design but also eliminates the need for computationally intensive high-dimensional matrix decomposition and fully-digital antenna array implementation. Numerical results substantiate the efficacy of our RIS-customized JSDM in not only achieving effective channel block-diagonalization but also in significantly enhancing the sum spectral efficiency for multi-UE transmissions.

title: Joint Phase and Power Optimization in SIM-Assisted NOMA Downlink Systems
authors: Ani Rosyidah, Hasriyasni Mandalika, Arnav Mukhopadhyay, Mayur Katwe, Keshav Singh, Cunhua Pan
abstract: Intelligent metasurfaces are emerging as a key technology for future wireless systems, enabling programmable control of electromagnetic wave propagation. Compared to conventional single-layer reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS), stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIM) introduce multiple reconfigurable layers to provide more flexible and precise beamforming. This paper investigates the integration of SIM into a downlink non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) system to improve spectral efficiency while maintaining low hardware complexity. The proposed system combines maximum ratio transmission (MRT) precoding at the base station, SIM-assisted analog beamforming, and NOMA-based power allocation. To maximize the system sum rate, we perform joint optimization of SIM phase shifts and user power levels through an alternating optimization (AO) framework, where each variable is updated iteratively while the other is held fixed. We evaluate three SIM-assisted strategies: NOMA, water-filling, and uniform power allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the SIM-NOMA configuration achieves the 40% sum rate improvements, outperforming the other schemes while leveraging the low-cost wave-domain processing capabilities of SIM.

title: Joint Position and Beamforming Optimization for Full-Duplex MIMO Systems with Position-Reconfigurable Antennas
authors: Chengjie Zhao, Tho Le-Ngoc
abstract: This paper investigates performance improvement of full-duplex (FD) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) communication systems through the integration of position reconfigurable antennas (PRAs). Using weighted sum-rate as the evaluation metric, we analyze a system where a base station simultaneously serves both downlink and uplink users, with both transmitters and receivers equipped with PRAs. To maximize the weighted sum-rate, we formulate a highly non-convex optimization problem subject to constraints on the reconfigurable region size, minimum inter-antenna distance, and transmit power. To tackle this challenge, we propose an alternating optimization framework that decomposes the original problem into sub-problems and solves them iteratively. Within this framework, fractional programming techniques are employed to decouple optimization variables from logarithmic and ratio terms, while a block successive upper-bound minimization approach addresses the non-convexity of PRA positioning. Simulation results confirm the performance gain achieved by incorporating PRAs into FD-MIMO systems and demonstrate the advantages of the proposed optimization algorithm.

title: Lightweight Multi-Task Deep Learning Model for Retinal Disease Classification and Vessel Segmentation
authors: Mariem Qaddour, Hanae Belmajdoub, Yassine Benjelloune Touimi, Khalid Minaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: We propose a lightweight multi-task deep learning architecture tailored for simultaneous retinal disease classification and blood vessel segmentation. Leveraging a shared Mo-bileNetV2 encoder, the model branches into task-specific heads for multi-class diabetic retinopathy (DR) grading and binary vessel segmentation. It is trained on the APTOS 2019 dataset for classification and the DRIVE dataset for segmentation using a label-masked, unified training strategy. The proposed model achieves 76.67% classification accuracy and a 0.5868 Dice score on segmentation, demonstrating competitive performance with significantly reduced computational complexity. Designed for real-time deployment on mobile devices, this model addresses the critical need for scalable, low-resource ophthalmic screening tools. Future work explores the integration of uncertainty estimation to enhance clinical interpretability and decision support.

title: Analytical Near-Field Beamforming With Reduced Power Pollution
authors: Peng Wang, Branislav M. Popovic
abstract: In downlink near-field communication systems, although the conventional maximum ratio transmission (MRT) based beamforming (BF) maximizes the received signal power at the targeted user equipment (UE), it suffers from a beam misfocus problem.
