To address this, a novel single-line ground fault detection criterion based on the Integer Factor (IF)-Approximate Derivative (AD) method is proposed for mine distribution networks containing VFDs. The original zero-sequence current signals-obtained via Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) decomposition under varying ground resistance conditions and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink-were downsampled and decomposed using the IF-AD method. This process yielded multiple signals with different sampling frequencies and orders. Subsequently, the energy entropy characteristics of these signals were calculated, and the weight ratios of these energy entropy characteristics under normal and fault states were computed to distinguish between operational modes. Finally, an established mining section distribution network model was implemented in MATLAB/Simulink. Various fault scenarios were simulated to validate the proposed criterion. Experimental results demonstrate that the extracted fault characteristics accurately identify both normal operation and single-line ground faults in low-voltage underground mine networks with VFDs.

title: GRAPPE: Game-Theoretic Approach for Precise Positioning in GNSS-Denied Environments
authors: Md Sadman Siraj, Eirini Eleni Tsiropoulou
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) are critical for enhancing the precise Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT). However, GNSS signals are often disrupted by interference, jamming, or spoofing, especially in harsh environments blocked by obstacles or by intentional intervention of malicious users. To address these challenges, we introduce GRAPPE solution, which is a game-theoretic approach for precise target positioning in GNSS-denied environments. GRAPPE leverages the Potential Game Theory to enable distributed nodes to collaboratively and autonomously determine the position of a target, such as an enemy in military scenarios or a victim in civilian search-and-rescue operations. We model the interactions among the nodes and provide the existence of a Pure Nash Equilibrium (PNE). Two learning-based algorithms, Strategic Optimization Dynamics (SOD) and Comprehensive Exploration Dynamics (CED), are designed to determine the PNE by mainly focusing on exploitation and exploration, respectively. Detailed simulation results demonstrate that GRAPPE outperforms existing PNT solutions in terms of superior accuracy and scalability.

title: Rethinking the Underexplored Role of Vision Encoder Selection in EEG-Based Visual Decoding
authors: Yebin Choi, Jun-Mo Kim, WooHyeok Choi, Songha Kim, Tae-Eui Kam
abstract: EEG-based visual decoding typically aligns neural signals with image embeddings extracted from pretrained vision encoders. However, prior work has predominantly relied on CLIP-based vision encoders, leaving the role of vision encoder selection in EEG–image alignment largely unexplored. In this study, we investigate how different vision encoders affect EEG-to-image alignment, focusing on how variations in visual representation spaces influence decoding performance. Specifically, we evaluate five vision encoders (CLIP, EVA02-CLIP, SigLIP2, DINOv2, iBOT) and four EEG encoders (EEGNet, NICE, ATM, CognitionCapturer) across two embedding dimensions, 768 and 1024. Alignment performance is assessed through retrieval accuracy on the THINGS-EEG dataset. Our results show that decoding performance depends not only on the EEG encoder architecture but also on the representational structure of the vision encoder. Models such as EVA02-CLIP generally yielded representations that aligned more effectively with EEG signals, while DINOv2 formed representations that were considerably less compatible. These results indicate that the choice of vision encoder is a critical factor in neural alignment and should be carefully considered in future EEG-based decoding frameworks.

title: AIM-SURE: AI-driven Multi-Scale Unified Robust SSB Channel Estimation in 5G and Beyond
authors: Adarsh Ravi, M J Siya, Satya Kumar Vankayala, Sukhdeep Singh, Preetam Kumar, Peter Moonki Hong
abstract: Accurate channel estimation is essential for reliable downlink synchronization (DLSync) in 5G and beyond wireless systems, especially during the initial access (IA) phase. This work focuses on synchronization signal blocks (SSBs), which play a crucial role in delivering system information from the base station (gNodeB) to user equipment (UE). We propose a deep learning-based approach using an inception-style neural network to estimate the channel across the SSB time-frequency grid. Our model outperforms traditional techniques such as demodulation reference signal (DMRS) interpolation and least squares (LS) estimation, especially under practical wireless conditions like multipath delay spread and Doppler shift. The proposed model achieves a bit error rate (BER) of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup> at an SNR of 20 dB, significantly better than the 3 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> BER of conventional methods. Moreover, we have observed 4-5 dB gain at high SNR with respect to LMMSE and LS estimators. These results demonstrate that our model offers more reliable and energy-efficient synchronization, even in challenging real-world environments.

title: Meta–cognitive Multi–scale Hierarchical Reasoning for Motor Imagery Decoding
authors: Si-Hyun Kim, Heon-Gyu Kwak, Byoung-Hee Kwon, Seong-Whan Lee
abstract: Brain–computer interface (BCI) aims to decode motor intent from noninvasive neural signals to enable control of external devices, but practical deployment remains limited by noise and variability in motor imagery (MI)–based electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. This work investigates a hierarchical and meta–cognitive decoding framework for four–class MI classification. We introduce a multi–scale hierarchical signal processing module that reorganizes backbone features into temporal multi– scale representations, together with an introspective uncertainty estimation module that assigns per–cycle reliability scores and guides iterative refinement. We instantiate this framework on three standard EEG backbones (EEGNet, ShallowConvNet, and DeepConvNet) and evaluate four–class MI decoding using the BCI Competition IV–2a dataset under a subject–independent setting. Across all backbones, the proposed components improve average classification accuracy and reduce inter–subject variance compared to the corresponding baselines, indicating increased robustness to subject heterogeneity and noisy trials. These results suggest that combining hierarchical multi–scale processing with introspective confidence estimation can enhance the reliability of MI–based BCI systems.

title: SCPT: A Spatio-Temporal-Request Computing Power Trading Framework Based on Discriminatory Auction Mechanism in Edge-Cloud Service Market
authors: Sixin Chen, Xiuhua Li, Jinlong Hao, Yingbo Wu, Xiaofei Wang, Victor C. M. Leung
abstract: In edge-cloud service market, users can access low-latency computing power and engage in dynamic trading of the resources. However, this paradigm inherently presents challenges such as market volatility, pricing rigidity, and inefficiency. To address these challenges, this paper designs a spatio-temporal-request computing power trading framework in the edge-cloud service market, SCPT. The framework utilizes a discriminatory auction mechanism for trading pricing and achieves dynamic request dispatching and resource procurement. Accounting for the spatio-temporal fluctuations in computational resource pricing and the unpredictability of user demands, we formulate a stochastic optimization problem aiming to minimize the intermediary’s long-term rental costs while ensuring personalized Quality of Experience (QoE) for users and propose an online algorithm leveraging Lyapunov optimization to address this issue. Experiments demonstrate that SCPT can achieve the lowest cost and improve market efficiency while meeting personalized QoE for users compared to the considered baselines.

title: On Graph Sparsification with Generative Model and Dual-Reward System for Generalized Tasks
authors: Yi-Ying Chen, Pin-Yen Lin, Tzu-Hsin Lin, Yi-Ling Chen
abstract: Graph sparsification is a vital technique that aims to reduce network complexity while preserving essential functionality. Traditional approaches often involve edge selection and deletion based on the given task. However, these methods tend to lack adaptability across diverse tasks. To address this limitation, we propose a novel framework called Graph Sparsification with Generative Model and Dual-Reward System for Generalized Tasks (GSGT). The GSGT framework employs the generative model to gradually comprehend the entire graph’s structure via random walks. The training of GSGT leverages the local reward obtained by evaluating local nodes of the enhanced graph, and also the global reward obtained by evaluating the synthesis graph. These rewards guide the random walk generation model to adapt to and optimize for various tasks. We further utilize the edge synthesis process that jointly considers graph connectivity and performance assessment to customize graph assembly according to specific task requirements. A notable feature of GSGT is the ability to generate artificial edges that do not exist in the original graph. These artificial edges can replace the existing weak edges and enhance the graph’s performance even beyond the original. This is a functionality that many traditional sparsification approaches find challenging to provide. The experimental results on three real-world datasets demonstrate that GSGT outperforms existing methods across various tasks, further affirming strong generalization capabilities and effectiveness of the proposed framework.

title: Gravity-Compensation Control of the Machine-Tool Vertical Axis Based on a Disturbance Observer and Linear-Parameter-Varying Control
authors: Xin Chen, Tao Huang, Jie Gong, Zhong Jiang, Xuecheng Li, Yele Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Mechanical Engineering, Intelligent Manufacturing, and Automation Technology (MEMAT)
abstract: To address the difficulties of dynamically balancing time-varying gravitational loads on the vertical axis of current multi-axis machine tools, as well as the nonlinear and time-varying pressure characteristics of pneumatic cylinders, this paper proposes an integrated control approach combining a disturbance observer and linear-parameter-varying modeling. The disturbance observer monitors load variations in real time; based on this, a linear time-varying dynamic model of the gravitycompensation cylinder is established, and a time-varying Linear Quadratic Regulator controller is designed to enhance the robustness of the vertical-axis system across different working positions. Experiments on a simulation platform demonstrate that the proposed method achieves adaptive balancing control under changing loads without additional sensors, thereby improving the operational stability of ultra-precision vertical axes.

title: Short-Term Scheduling Model for Cascade Hydropower Integrated with Dynamic Programming
authors: Xinke Qin, Shijun Tian, Xian Zhang, Ziming Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Hydropower systems, among the most complex in water resources management, present significant challenges in optimal scheduling due to inherent nonlinear constraints. This study proposes a short-term scheduling approach for cascade hydropower integrated with dynamic programming. First, dynamic programming transforms the unit-level NQH characteristics into plant-level NQH characteristics. The results feed into the subsequent cascade hydropower scheduling model. Then, within this model, three-dimensional interpolation linearizes the nonlinear constraints of the hydropower generation function, converting them into a form directly solvable by optimization solvers. Finally, simulation validation was conducted using a simplified case study based on actual hydropower data from Yunnan province. Comparative analysis with two linearization methods from existing literature demonstrates that the proposed approach ensures computational efficiency and accuracy in short-term scheduling scenarios.

title: High-Precision Vehicle Key Localization System Based on Multi-Anchor Collaboration
authors: Ranran Zhang, Ze Li, Zengshan Tian, Lingxia Li, Heng Wang, Kaikai Liu, Shuliang Gui
abstract: With the continuous development of internet of things and smart vehicles, the localization function of vehicle keys has become increasingly important. Through precise localization, vehicle can enable automatic unlocking and vehicle ignition, further enhancing security and convenience. Therefore, this paper proposes a vehicle key localization system based on multi-anchor collaboration. The system first utilizes the orthogonal matching pursuit algorithm to estimate the time of flight from the channel state information, thereby achieving ranging. Then, by analyzing the phase under Line-of-Sight (LOS) and Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) anchors, LOS/NLOS identification is performed based on phase differences. To achieve high-precision localization of the vehicle key, we combine LOS and NLOS anchors to accurately determine the specific location area of the vehicle key around the vehicle. After determining the spatial area of the vehicle key, we implement an innovative direct position estimation algorithm to determine its location. This algorithm achieves high-precision localization even when only two anchors are available. Finally, the system is tested in a parking lot using a software defined radio platform to verify its performance and accuracy. The experimental results show that the proposed algorithm not only improves localization accuracy but also enhances computational efficiency, showcasing its superiority and practicality in real-world scenarios.

title: Efficient Resource Allocation for MU-MISO Image Semantic Communication Systems
authors: Haoqi Che, Jianhua Tang
abstract: Semantic communication, as one of the key technologies for next-generation wireless communications, shows great potential for improving transmission effectiveness. In this paper, we investigate efficient resource allocation methods in the multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) image semantic communication system. To this end, we first construct a function in closed-form for semantic similarity accuracy using a data regression approach. A multi-user weighted sum semantic similarity maximization problem is then formulated, by jointly optimizing the semantic downsampling module and transmit beamforming, following both the transmit power and transmission delay constraints. Due to the finite and dependent nature of the original problem, we decompose the problem into multiple transmit beamforming problems in parallel. For the transmit beamforming problem, we use the successive convex approximation (SCA) technique and transform the problem into a second-order cone programming. A special case study is further discussed to reduce the complexity of transmit beamforming problem as a linear programming. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithm compared to conventional beamforming methods.

title: Research on Adaptive Control Strategy for Multi Branch Parallel High-Power Hydrogen Production Power Supply
authors: Derong Lin, Hao Liu, Yinghui Zhang, Wenjin Duan, Rui He
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Evolution and Power Engineering: Transition, Intelligence, and Autonomy (EEPE-TIA)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of new energy installed capacity, high-power hydrogen production power supplies for water electrolysis require multi-unit parallel connection to satisfy high power demand. It leads to the new issue of power balance control and reactive power compensation in multi-unit parallel systems. This paper first analyzes the causes of system power module imbalance and grid voltage drop based on the proposed topology. It establishes the circuit equivalent model for power imbalance in parallel hydrogen production power supplies and grid-connected rectifiers, and then analyzes the characteristics of parallel balance and grid connection under different control modes of the hydrogen production power supply. Secondly, a solution is proposed using communication methods to achieve adaptive system impedance adjustment and adaptive reactive power compensation for the hydrogen production power supply. Finally, a simulation model of the parallel hydrogen production power supply is built to verify the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Efficient Radio Resource Management in C-V2X with Federated Graph Neural Networks
authors: Hefu Li, Yunlong Lu, Yueyue Dai, Zhangdui Zhong, Yan Zhang
abstract: The rapid proliferation of cellular vehicle-to-everything (C-V2X) communications calls for efficient radio resource management (RRM). Effective C-V2X systems require both ultra-low latency for V2V safety communications and high throughput for V2I services. Current systems struggle with coordinating heterogeneous requirements under rapidly changing channel conditions. To address this challenge, we propose a new framework that integrates Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with decentralized federated learning(DFL). Our approach embeds a dynamic graph representation into the vehicular network, where communication links are modeled as graph nodes. To adapt to rapidly changing topologies, we adopt the enhanced Graph Sample and Aggregation (GraphSAGE) for scalable neighborhood aggregation. Additionally, we apply unsupervised primal-dual learning to parameterize RRM policies based on instantaneous channel conditions. To improve generalization across network, we introduce a federated model aggregation strategy that employs channel-state-aligned isomorphic GNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to traditional GNN-based benchmark solutions, our framework achieves near-optimal system throughput while maintaining low computational complexity.

title: Exploring the Impact of Digital Nudges-Insights from Social Media and Gen Z
authors: Amira Ben Mohamed, Ibrahim Bounhas, Hatem Ghrab
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: In the dynamic world of digital technology, User eXperience design plays a pivotal role in shaping human behavior. This research explores the intersection of interface design and social psychology, aiming to unveil the enduring consequences of digital nudges and their antithesis. The study explores how this design components extend beyond our screens, influencing not only our immediate actions but also our long-term social and psychological behavior and health. Addressing a notable research gap, this study moves beyond treating Generation Z as a monolithic group and instead explores differences within this cohort. It examines the link between frequent positive responses to digital nudges on social media and attention problems among Tunisian youth. Survey data show that users who check for updates more often are significantly more likely to report difficulty concentrating after using social media, especially for the younger users. The results further highlight significant differences within this group in their responsiveness to digital nudges; they also reveal a potential cognitive cost of repeated engagement with nudge-driven platforms, underscoring implications for e-health and educational settings, and suggest the need for more mindful digital design to support wellbeing and learning outcomes.

title: Thermal Comfort Prediction in Green Buildings Using Machine Learning
authors: Vani Soni, Deivangh Grover, Rachna Behl
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Energy optimization plays a vital role in green buildings by maintaining environmental sustainability along with economic efficiency. However, green building is a concept that aims to minimize energy consumption without compromising comfort. This research explores the application of Machine Learning (ML), specifically Random Forest (RF) model to predict thermal comfort of an occupant within a building using three categories: hot, comfortable, and cold. The authors have utilized ASHRAE dataset, consisting of over 70 factors, such as temperature, humidity, etc. Systematic data preprocessing along with data cleaning, z- normalization, and handling missing values has been applied in this work. This not only refined the data but also helped in achieving better accuracy. Further improvements were made by applying hyperparameter tuning and data balancing with SMOTE. The accuracy improved significantly from 0.5483 to 0.8634. This demonstrated that small, clean, and balanced datasets can outperform larger ones.

title: MauBa: A Multi-Agent Coordination Framework for Vision-Language-Guided Zero-Shot Control of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Yulin Zhu, Zihan Zhao, Shilong Zhang, Junzhe Sun, Keping Yu
abstract: While prompt-based methods have shown initial potential for enabling large language models (LLMs) to control unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), they suffer from limited flexibility, inadequate visual perception, and low code generation accuracy—often leading to hallucinated outputs due to static templates and rigid API wrappers. To address these limitations, we propose MauBa, a multi-agent coordination framework for zero-shot vision-language UAVs control. MauBa comprises three collaborative agents—Supervisor, Coder, and Tracker—that jointly decompose and execute natural language instructions. The Supervisor manages task delegation and dialogue context; the Coder utilizes a vectorized API knowledge base with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for precise code synthesis; and the Tracker performs object detection and spatial localization via visual grounding. Without any fine-tuning, MauBa achieves task success rates of 93.3% for language-action and 83.3% for vision-language tasks in the AirSim simulation environment, significantly outperforming prompt-based and single-agent baselines in both accuracy and control robustness. Ablation studies further validate the essential role of task coordination and knowledge retrieval in ensuring reliable UAV decision-making. These findings suggest that modular multi-agent collaboration offers a promising pathway for scalable multimodal reasoning in real-world autonomous systems.

title: EdgeFLow: Serverless Federated Learning via Sequential Model Migration in Edge Networks
authors: Yuchen Shi, Qijun Hou, Pingyi Fan, Khaled B. Letaief
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a transformative distributed learning paradigm in the era of Internet of Things (IoT), reconceptualizing data processing methodologies. However, FL systems face significant communication bottlenecks due to inevitable client-server data exchanges and long-distance transmissions. This work presents EdgeFLow, an innovative FL framework that redesigns the system topology by replacing traditional cloud servers with sequential model migration between edge base stations. By conducting model aggregation and propagation exclusively at edge clusters, EdgeFLow eliminates cloud-based transmissions and substantially reduces global communication overhead. We provide rigorous convergence analysis for EdgeFLow under non-convex objectives and non-IID data distributions, extending classical FL convergence theory. Experimental results across various configurations validate the theoretical analysis, demonstrating that EdgeFLow achieves comparable accuracy improvements while significantly reducing communication costs. As a systemic architectural innovation for communication-efficient FL, EdgeFLow establishes a foundational framework for future developments in IoT and edge-network learning systems.

title: Spatial Degrees of Freedom in Distance Domain of Continuous Aperture for Broadside case in Near-Field Region
authors: Son T. Duong, Tho Le-Ngoc
abstract: Extremely large apertures in the near-field region unlock additional spatial resources in the distance domain, which enables the spatial multiplexing of multiple users even when they share the same angular direction—a capability unattainable in the far-field regime. A fundamental question remains unanswered: "What is the spatial degree of freedom of spatial multiplexing in the distance domain?"To address this, we investigate the spatial degree of freedom (DoF) in the distance domain of a large continuous aperture by considering the line-of-sight (LoS) channel between the aperture and a linear array, whose elements lie in the same direction but are located at different distances relative to the aperture. For simplicity of analysis, we consider the case where the linear array is in the broadside of the aperture. By reformulating the channel as an integral operator with a Hermitian convolution kernel, we derive a closed-form expression for the spatial DoF via the Fourier transform. Analytical and simulation results reveal that the spatial DoF in the distance domain of the aperture is predominantly determined by the aperture’s extreme boundaries rather than its detailed shape.

title: Comparative Study of Adaptive Low-Precision Floating-Point and Fixed-Point Quantization for CNNs on FPGA-Oriented Architectures
authors: Kawthar Dellel, Emanuel Trabes, Hassene Faiedh, Carlos Valderrama
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: Quantization is a key enabler for deploying convolutional neural networks (CNNs) on field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), where energy efficiency is tightly coupled to numerical precision. While fixed-point formats dominate FPGA-based quantization due to their hardware simplicity, low-precision floating-point (LPFP) has recently emerged as a compelling alternative, offering a larger dynamic range at comparable or even lower bit-widths. In this work, we conduct a comparative analysis of adaptive LPFP and fixed-point quantization in the context of FPGA-oriented CNN deployment. We integrate both formats into a U-Net architecture for monocular depth estimation on the DIODE dataset, where each layer autonomously learns its precision configuration during training—bit-width for fixed-point and exponent–mantissa allocation for floating-point. The study reveals that the adaptive floating-point model consistently achieves comparable accuracy while converging to a lower average bit-width than its fixed-point counterpart. Although the evaluation is performed in a software training environment, these findings indicate that LPFP quantization can offer a promising balance between precision flexibility and efficiency for future FPGA implementations.

title: Manifestation-Aware Contrastive Learning for Cross-Domain Tomato Disease Classification with Limited Data
authors: Muhammad Shafay, Divya Velayudhan, Sarra Ben Abbes, Taimur Hassan, Irfan Hussain, Naoufel Werghi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Data-Driven Analytics And Intelligent Systems (ADACIS)
abstract: Agricultural disease classification remains challenging due to limited training data, high visual variability, and poor cross-domain generalization between curated and field conditions. Existing vision-language models often fail to capture the fine-grained, multi-scale symptom patterns critical for accurate diagnosis. We propose Manifestation-Aware Contrastive Learning (MACL), a vision-language framework tailored to the complexities of tomato disease classification. MACL constructs dual manifestation bags by pairing global and local visual crops with diverse, symptom-focused textual descriptions. It employs hierarchical contrastive learning with separTate branches for global context and localized symptoms, aligning multi-scale visual and semantic features to learn robust representations from limited data without additional annotation. Extensive experiments across four unseen datasets spanning both curated and field conditions demonstrate that MACL significantly outperforms state-of-the-art vision-language baselines, achieving strong cross-domain generalization to both seen and unseen disease classes.

title: Message-Passing Algorithm for Pseudoinverse Computation in Graph-Based Decoding of Noisy IRSA
authors: Po-Yu Yeh, Cheng-Shang Chang
abstract: The computation of pseudoinverse matrices is critical in various communication and signal processing applications, especially in decoding schemes such as parallel decoding in Irregular Repetition Slotted ALOHA (IRSA) with noise. Traditional methods like singular value decomposition (SVD) are computationally intensive for large, sparse matrices. To address this issue, we propose a novel message-passing algorithm leveraging the dynamic programming principle to efficiently compute the pseudoinverse matrix of acyclic bipartite graphs, guaranteeing exact solutions. For cyclic graphs, we introduce modifications to handle non-orthogonal message propagation and implement a revised convergence criterion, resulting in accurate approximations or exact solutions upon convergence. Numerical results demonstrate that our algorithm converges faster and more reliably compared to Gaussian belief propagation (GaBP), particularly for cyclic SIC-decodable graphs, highlighting its potential in large-scale networked systems.

title: Edge IoT-based Voice-Activated Health Monitoring System
authors: Masoud Askariraad, Marc Jayson Baucas, Stefano Gregori, Petros Spachos
abstract: This paper presents a voice-activated health monitoring system implemented using a Raspberry Pi. The prototype consists of two nodes: a medical sensor node with the user and a processing node, which communicates via a radio link. This system relies on speech recognition as the basis of its voice-activated design. It uses Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients for feature extraction and a dynamic time-warping algorithm for keyword recognition and comparing reference patterns. The system offers a stable, secure, and cloud-independent solution, achieving a keyword spotting accuracy of 95%.

title: Open RAN-Enabled Vehicular Edge Computing with Dual-Timescale Robust Offloading
authors: Mohsen Tajallifar, Nizar Zorba, Hamid Saeedi, Nader Mokari
abstract: Vehicular edge computing (VEC) is a critical enabler of low-latency and computation-intensive vehicular applications by offloading tasks from vehicles to edge servers. However, the dynamic nature of vehicular networks introduces significant uncertainty in task characteristics and network conditions. This paper proposes a robust task offloading scheme for VEC within the open radio access network (O-RAN) architecture. The proposed scheme integrates large-timescale computational resource allocation (CRA) with small-timescale task partitioning and radio resource allocation (RRA) using O-RAN’s hierarchical control framework. Our scheme minimizes the network-wide resources under latency constraints that are subjected to demand uncertainty. We employ the cutting-set method to address the demand uncertainty in the large-timescale CRA. We obtain a closed-form solution to the optimal task partitioning problem and provide a heuristic approach for the small-timescale RRA. Simulation results show that the small-timescale RRA succeeds to counteract the demand uncertainty at the large-timescale CRA, that is, no outage occurs when demands are in the assumed uncertainty set, whereas the non-robust scheme exhibits as high as 50% outage probability. Moreover, our slotted scheme consumes about 50% less bandwidth than the conventional non-slotted robust solution.

title: An AES Based Physical Layer Message Authentication and Encryption Scheme
authors: Chunjiang Lai, Yan Gao, Boyang Zhou, Shihan Fang, Jingguo Bi, Haipeng Peng, Lixiang Li
abstract: In this paper, we study the physical layer message authentication and encryption scheme for wireless networks, based on the Advanced Encryption Standard(AES)-based authentication encryption scheme. Specifically, we first propose an efficient and feasible authentication encryption scheme built upon the GCM-SIV mode and utilizing the AES algorithm. Secondly, we propose a novel physical layer security scheme that seamlessly integrates precoding-based message authentication and encryption with the AGSP algorithm by strategically leveraging the unique characteristics of wireless channels. By integrating encryption and authentication at the physical layer, our protocol offers a promising approach to scalable and secure communication in the 6G era.

title: Terahertz Spatial Wireless Channel Modeling with Radio Radiance Field
authors: John Song, Lihao Zhang, Feng Ye, Haijian Sun
abstract: Terahertz (THz) communication is a key enabler for 6G systems, offering ultra-wide bandwidth and unprecedented data rates. However, THz signal propagation differs significantly from lower-frequency bands due to severe free space path loss, minimal diffraction and specular reflection, and prominent scattering, making conventional channel modeling and pilot-based estimation approaches inefficient. In this work, we investigate the feasibility of applying radio radiance field (RRF) framework to the THz band. This method reconstructs a continuous RRF using visual-based geometry and sparse THz RF measurements, enabling efficient spatial channel state information (Spatial-CSI) modeling without dense sampling. We first build a fine simulated THz scenario, then we reconstruct the RRF and evaluate the performance in terms of both reconstruction quality and effectiveness in THz communication, showing that the reconstructed RRF captures key propagation paths with sparse training samples. Our findings demonstrate that RRF modeling remains effective in the THz regime and provides a promising direction for scalable, low-cost spatial channel reconstruction in future 6G networks.

title: Research on innovative applications of intelligent economic management systems
authors: Jin Hao, Peng Yan
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Information Economy, Data Modeling and Cloud Computing
abstract: None

title: Digital Transformation Enables Green Production in Manufacturing Enterprises: The Mediating Role of Dynamic Capabilities
authors: Chengliang Wang, Meiyu Jiang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Information Economy, Data Modeling and Cloud Computing
abstract: None

title: Research on Typical Job Competencies of New Energy Vehicle Manufacturers Based on LDA Topic Model
authors: Yixian Zhang, Zhuojie Li, Fangyun Chen
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Information Economy, Data Modeling and Cloud Computing
abstract: None

title: Performance Analysis of Pinching Antenna Systems (PASS) in Uplink Transmission
authors: Tianwei Hou, Yuanwei Liu, Arumugam Nallanathan
abstract: Pinching antenna (PA) is a flexible antenna composed of a waveguide and multiple dielectric particles, which is capable of reconfiguring wireless channels intelligently in line-of-sight links. By leveraging the unique features of PAs, we exploit the uplink (UL) transmission in pinching antenna systems (PASS). To comprehensively evaluate the performance gains of PASS in UL transmissions, multiple PAs are deployed for a single user, namely MPSU. The positions of PAs are optimized to obtain the maximal channel gains in the considered scenarios. For the MPSU and SPSU scenarios, by applying the optimized position of PAs, closed-form expressions for analytical, asymptotic and approximated ergodic rate are derived. Our results demonstrate the following key insights: i) The PA distribution follows an asymmetric non-uniform distribution in the MPSU scenario; ii) Optimizing PA positions significantly enhances the ergodic sum rate performance.

title: Modeling the Influence of Low-Level Jet Height on Wake of Horizontal Axis Wind Turbine
authors: Jianmei Zhang, Zhenzhen Zhang, Ruixiao Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy Evolution and Power Engineering: Transition, Intelligence, and Autonomy (EEPE-TIA)
abstract: To explore the impact of low-level jets on the wake characteristics of horizontal axis wind turbines, a three-dimensional pulsating wind field was constructed using jet theory and the IEC Karman spectrum method. This wind field served as the inflow condition for a 5 MW wind turbine, facilitating the study of how variations in the height of low-level jets affect wake performance. The findings indicate that the axial velocity deficit in the wake is minimized when the low-level jet aligns with the wind turbine's hub center height. Conversely, the deficit is maximized when the low-level jet is at the lowest point of the wind turbine rotor's sweep.

title: Optimization and Simulation Analysis of Power System Parameters for Pure Electric Mining Heavy Trucks
authors: Jia Miao, Keqilao Meng, Rihan Hai, Yonglai Zhao, Rongming Xu, Hu Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Pure electric mining trucks are key equipment for green transformationin mining operations, with their powertrain parameters directly impacting overall vehicle performance. Based on vehicle design specifications, this study completed parameter matching for key components including the drive motor, power battery, and transmission system. A vehicle model was established using CRUISE software, with simulations verifying that power performance and economy meet requirements. Building upon this, Isight was integrated to optimize the transmission gear ratio, effectively enhancing metrics such as maximum speed, acceleration performance, hill-climbing capability, and cruising range. This provides a reference for designing electric mining truck powertrains.

title:
Thermal-Hydraulic Simulation of Supercritical CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Pipelines Based on the PR and GERG-2008 Equations
authors: Yaran Bu, Ming Zhang, Jinghua Chen, Pengcheng Li, Xin Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Supercritical/dense-phase carbon dioxide (CO2) pipeline transportation is a crucial component in Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technology. While the PengRobinson (PR) equation of state is commonly used for predicting CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> properties in pipeline simulations, the GERG-2008 model is recognized for its higher accuracy in obtaining certain critical properties. In this study, equations of state (EoSs) based on both PR and GERG-2008 are developed; and a thermo-hydraulic model is built through numerical discretization of the governing equations, which calls for different EoS models to calculate fluid properties. Validation against the commercial software OLGA shows pressure and temperature errors of the thermo-hydraulic model within 0.2 %. Computational performance analysis reveals that a single call to the PR and GERG-2008 models takes 0.0014 seconds and 0.035 seconds, respectively, while the full thermohydraulic simulations require 83 seconds and 2440 secondsindicating that the GERG-2008 approach is currently impractical for engineering applications due to excessive computational time. Comparisons between the models show a pressure drop error of 11 % and a maximum temperature error of 4.2 %, demonstrating that physical property calculations significantly affect thermohydraulic results. Thus, accelerating the GERG-2008-based thermal-hydraulic model is essential. The models developed in this work provide a foundation for enhancing the simulation tools necessary for advancing CCUS projects.

title: Simulation Analysis and Application Research on the Selection of Variable-Speed Pumped Storage Units in High-Renewable Power Grids
authors: Yuhong Chang, Mingyi Yue, Zhaoxin Song, Tingting Wang, Jiejun Zhao, Xiaodi Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: In high-penetration renewable energy power systems, enhancing energy absorption capacity and operational economy remains a critical challenge. This study focuses on a wind-solar-hydropower complementary system, and conducts comparative analyses of pumped storage units with different types and configuration scales (fixed-speed, doubly-fed variable-speed, and full-converter variable-speed; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0}, \mathbf{2}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}$</tex> units). A simulation model is developed, and a multi-attribute evaluation framework covering both technical performance and economic benefits is established. The entropy-weighted TOPSIS method is then applied for optimal unit selection. Furthermore, the proposed approach is validated using the actual operational data of a pumped storage power station from January to June 2025. Results indicate that, compared with fixed-speed units, variable-speed configurations can reduce curtailment rates by 3.49 %. Their flexible operation increases renewable energy absorption by approximately 147 million kWh, resulting in an additional revenue of about 13.42 million RMB. These findings demonstrate the engineering feasibility and promotion value of the proposed scheme. This research provides a systematic and replicable technical pathway for pumped storage unit selection and coordinated operation strategies in high-penetration renewable energy power systems.

title: Comprehensive Optimization Analysis of Hydrogen Production Performance in the Biomass Steam Reforming Process
authors: Shidan Chi, Xin Yu, Yan Liang, Anxin Chen, Xiaochen Wang, Lei Zhang, Xiaoxu Zhang, Yan Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Biomass steam reforming for hydrogen production represents a critical technological pathway for the efficient conversion of renewable energy and the clean production of hydrogen, offering advantages in carbon neutrality and resource recycling. This study employed thermodynamic equilibrium analysis to systematically investigate the hydrogen production performance and energy consumption characteristics of the sorption-enhanced steam reforming (SE-SR) process under key operating parameters such as temperature, steam-to-carbon ratio (S/C), and calcium-to-carbon ratio <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{Ca} / \mathrm{C})$</tex>. The results demonstrated that SE-SR, by utilizing in-situ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> adsorption via CaO to overcome reaction equilibrium limitations, exhibited superior hydrogen production capacity and carbon suppression effects in the medium-to-low temperature range <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(550 \sim 700{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$</tex>. Under optimal operating conditions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$650{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{bar}, \mathrm{S} / \mathrm{C}=2$</tex>), the hydrogen yield reached <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.65 \text{Nm}^{3} / \text{kg}$</tex>, with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}_{2}$</tex> volume fraction exceeding 77%. The specific energy consumption for hydrogen production <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{Q}_{\mathrm{D} / \mathrm{H}_{2}}\right)$</tex> was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3482.3 \text{kJ} / \text{Nm}^{3}$</tex>, and the biomass consumption rate (YBiomass/ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>) was only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.64 \text{kg} / \mathbf{N m}^{\mathbf{3}}$</tex>. Compared to conventional steam reforming, SE-SR significantly enhanced carbon conversion efficiency and system thermal efficiency, while simultaneously reducing biomass consumption and energy input. This study provides a theoretical foundation for the design and operation of green hydrogen production systems from biomass.

title: Multi-Objective Bayesian Approach for Optimizing Subjective-Objective Performance of Tactile Internet
authors: Hao Xiang, Tong Zhang, Jiayuan Chen, Changyan Yi
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-objective Bayesian approach to optimize the end-to-end (E2E) performance of network function virtualization (NFV)-based tactile Internet (TI) by balancing subjective and objective performances. The system aims to deploy virtual network functions (VNFs) via middleware (e.g., servers, switches) to establish service function chains (SFCs), enabling bidirectional communication between tactile users and teleoperators, accelerating service deployment, and facilitating immersive tactile interaction requests (e.g., in the Metaverse). To meet the demands of an immersive user experience, we explore tailored E2E performance metrics and formulate a hybrid black-white box optimization problem. Addressing the uncertainty in subjective feedback (e.g., non-reproducible user ratings), the proposed approach jointly optimizes wireless resource allocation and SFC scheduling for bidirectional uplink/downlink communication in NFV-based TI, reducing E2E delay and enhancing user satisfaction. Simulations demonstrate the superiority of the proposed solution over existing approaches.

title: RadioVAE: Generating Probabilistic Radio Map via Variational Autoencoder with UNet
authors: Pan Yang, Nan Zhang, Zezhong Zhang, Junting Chen, Kai Chen, Shuguang Cui
abstract: Radio environment mapping in urban scenarios presents significant challenges due to the complex interplay of multipath propagation, shadowing effects, and heterogeneous urban morphology. We present RadioVAE, a novel probabilistic framework that generates high-fidelity radio maps with uncertainty quantification through a Variational Autoencoder (VAE). The model addresses the critical limitations of deterministic neural networks by learning a structured latent space that explicitly encodes the posterior distribution of propagation features conditioned on urban topology and transmitter characteristics. A UNet-based feature extractor enables simultaneous modeling of both fine-scale spatial variations and city-wide propagation patterns, while KL-divergence regularization ensures physically plausible predictions. The generative process incorporates urban morphological constraints and transmitter parameters to accurately reconstruct complex propagation fields. Experimental results demonstrate that RadioVAE significantly outperforms existing deterministic methods in both prediction accuracy and uncertainty calibration, particularly in challenging urban environments with complex spatial correlations.

title: Design and Efficacy of a Spray-Cooled Device for Marine Exhaust Heat Reduction
authors: Bin Fang, Tong Wei, Tong Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Addressing the challenge of high-temperature marine exhaust emissions, this study develops a Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) framework to model multiphase (gas-liquid) flow and interfacial heat/mass transfer within exhaust plumes, utilizing water spray cooling for thermal management. The EulerLagrange particle tracking method was employed to numerically analyze the intricate water-gas interactions. Model validation against experimental data confirmed its accuracy. Results demonstrate that under high-temperature, high-speed exhaust conditions, smaller spray droplet diameters substantially reduce evaporation time and enhance cooling efficiency. Counter-current spray injection intensifies the heat transfer at the water-gas interface, prolongs droplet residence within the cooling zone, and thereby im-proves evaporative cooling effectiveness. Although higher spray flow rates slow individual droplet evaporation rates, the overall exhaust outlet temperature remains effectively controlled. Through multi-parameter optimization, the spray cooling strategy achieved a significant temperature drop of 53 % in the exhaust plume. This study elucidates the mechanism of phase-change (water evaporation) cooling in marine exhausts. The pro-posed CFD-optimized spray cooling approach offers an engineering-compatible and cost-effective solution for mitigating thermal pollution and enhancing energy efficiency in marine operations, contributing to greener and more sustainable maritime practices with direct implications for reducing the thermal footprint of vessels on aquatic environments.

title: Quantum-Inspired Feature Selection with CNN-SVM Fusion for Pest Identification in Cloud Systems
authors: V. Elanangai, A. Sevuga Pandian, Sumanth Bodhuluri, B. Gopinathan, Karthikayen A, S. Shenbaga Ezhil
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Background: Timely, correct pest identification is fundamental to agricultural monitoring and crop protection. in cloud-based systems managing large data volumes, conventional machine learning methods disappoint in terms of feature optimization and decision fusion. Methodology: This work presents a Convolutional Neural Network-Support Vector Machine (CNN-SVM) Fusion approach combined with a Quantum-Inspired Feature Selection (QIFS) framework intended to identify pests in cloud-based systems. By mimicking the behavior of quantum systems, QIFS is used to maximize the efficacy of the search process by optimizing the selection of pertinent image features. Deep feature extraction is done using CNN; SVM serve as the decision layer, which improves the classification robustness. Its combination guarantees that the model can effectively capture decision boundaries as well as hierarchical patterns. Result and discussion: When compared to conventional CNN, SVM, and PCA-SVM models, the proposed QIFS-CNN-SVM model outperforms them in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Experiments run on a benchmark pest dataset yielded these results. With an accuracy of 96.7 %, the proposed approach indicates its potential application in cloud-based agricultural systems in a comprehensive manner.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Deep Learning Model for the Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy Using Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Akshay B M, Ananya N R, Anusha A K, Apeksha J N, Sarumathi, Minal K
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of preventable blindness, and early detection is essential for effective treatment. This paper proposes a convolutional neural network (CNN) is proposed for accurate classification of DR severity levels, with a target accuracy of 98 %. Transfer learning models such as DenseNet121, InceptionV3, and Xception, along with machine learning methods including SVM and RNNs, were evaluated. Xception achieved 82 % accuracy, while the proposed CNN outperformed existing methods, reaching 95 % accuracy on a diverse dataset and near-perfect performance in binary classification. To support real-time diagnosis, a user- friendly platform is being developed to enable instant retinal image analysis. The integration of explainable AI techniques, particularly Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), further enhances model interpretability and supports clinical decision-making. This research highlights the potential of deep learning in DR detection and provides practical insights into optimizing classification techniques for real-world medical applications.

title: Trust-Based Dynamic Node Security Monitoring: HMM-Driven Malicious Node Detection in Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Luxing Zhang, Jun Du, Xiangwang Hou, Wei Men, Minrui Xu, Yong Ren
abstract: Underwater acoustic sensor networks (UASNs) play a key role in ocean resource exploration and complex underwater tasks. However, the open acoustic channel makes them vulnerable to malicious node attacks. Therefore, accurately identifying attack nodes in harsh channels and adapting to their mobility presents a significant challenge. To address these issues, we adopt a meandering ocean current mobility model to describe node movement and construct a hidden Markov model (HMM) along with link transmission loss to characterize the unstable underwater acoustic channel. By monitoring the forwarding behavior of neighboring nodes and combining HMM state inference, we propose a trust model based on a subjective logic framework with dynamic topology updates to detect malicious nodes. It considers variable weights to assess improper node behavior and dynamically updates trustworthiness based on both historical trust and arrival strategies of new and old nodes. Simulation results indicate that the proposed method effectively identifies malicious nodes with attack intensities exceeding 0.38, and for intensities above 0.6, it achieves over 90% identification accuracy and adapts well to dynamic environmental mobility.

title: Research on the Operation Boundary and Flexible Resource Quantification of Regional Integrated Energy System Based on OPF-MIP Collaborative Optimization
authors: Jiaqi Zhang, Lihao Chen, Binbin Cao, Song Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: Under the background of global energy transition and the “dual carbon” goals, the efficient operation of Regional Integrated Energy Systems (RIES) relies on the precise characterization of the dynamic operation boundaries of the system and the quantitative assessment of the potential for flexible resource regulation. This paper proposes a method for modeling the operation boundary and quantifying flexible resources of regional integrated energy systems that integrates Optimal Power Flow (OPF) and Mixed Integer Programming (MIP). By constructing a random OPF model to depict the dynamic operation boundary and combining MIP to precisely quantify the regulation capabilities of flexible resources such as energy storage, demand response, and distributed energy, a three-dimensional evaluation system of “intensity - reserve rate - cost” is established. Empirical evidence in a certain industrial park in Shenyang shows that this method reduces the risk of system over-limit by 82.4% and lowers the rate of wind and solar power curtailment by 58.5%. This research provides theoretical tools and technical support for the collaborative scheduling of multiple market entities.

title: Verilog-Driven Simulation and RTL Architecture of the SPECK Cipher for High-Performance Cryptography in VLSI Systems
authors: Shivaputra, Pavithra G.
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: In order to ensure data security in resources like wireless sensor networks, built-in systems, and Internet of Things devices, cryptography is essential, which is the background. The NSA created the Speck algorithm in the early part of 2000's as a lightweight encryption block that strikes a compromise between security and effectiveness. The design, simulation, and Verilog implementation of the RTL algorithm are the main objectives of this project. This work's main motivation & objective are creating an efficient Verilog code and using simulation to confirm its accuracy. In order to optimize area, performance, and decryption, the design incorporates key expansion, encryption, and decryption procedures. Testing shots are used to mimic and confirm the validity of the verilog-based design. Through the effective and scalable implementation of Speck-based verilog, appropriate for IoT and built-in systems, this study advances current hardware security research. Expressions for indexes Verilog, design RTL, encryption, key spot, expansion, simulation, light cryptography, decryption are used for problem statement.

title: Harnessing Topological Semantics for Network Topology Diagram Retrieval
authors: Liangyun Sun, Yanfang Zhang, Yang Wei, Zhipeng Zou, Shengwei Zhong, Chen Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Network topology diagram retrieval aims to retrieve the topology diagrams with both visual and topological similarities to a query diagram within the domain of network science and engineering, which can be viewed as a specific image retrieval task. However, current image retrieval approaches are mostly designed for natural images which typically identify the similar candidates by measuring the similarity of visual features. Therefore, these approaches are inherently limited in retrieving network topology diagrams, as they struggle to effectively capture the crucial topological semantics (i.e., the semantics characterized by device roles, connection types, and network structures) embedded in network topology diagrams. To address this issue, we propose the Network Topology-aware Retrieval Framework (NTRF) by incorporating network domain knowledge, which emphasizes the functionality conveyed by the topological semantics to enhance retrieval performance. To be specific, beyond the traditional visual retrieval module, we design a Network Topology Encoder (NTE) to capture the topological semantics by simultaneously representing the device roles, connection types, and network structures. Furthermore, we introduce a SubGraph-aware Re-ranking Module (SGRM) to refine the ranking of candidates, which pays attention to the core diagram regions with significant topological semantics. Intensive experimental results on the collected dataset based on Huawei’s Product Documentation demonstrate that our NTRF outperforms state-of-the-art image retrieval methods by 2.75%, 6.19%, and 9.97% in terms of Recall@1, Recall@5, and Recall@10, respectively.

title: Single and Multi-Frequency Path Loss Models for Indoor Hotspot Scenario Based on Measurements Conducted at 6.75, 16.95, 28, 73 and 142 GHz
authors: Hitesh Poddar, Akhileswar Chowdary
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive derivation of single and multi-frequency large-scale path loss model parameters for the close-in (CI) free space reference distance, CI free space reference distance with cross-polarization (CIX), floating-intercept (FI), CI free space reference distance with frequency-dependent path loss exponent (CIF), CI free space reference distance with frequency-dependent path loss exponent and cross-polarization (CIFX), alpha-beta-gamma (ABG), and alpha-beta-gamma with cross-polarization (ABGX) models for specific frequencies and across frequency ranges of 7–24 GHz, 0.5–100 GHz, and 0.5–150 GHz. The analysis is based on extensive real-world measurements conducted by NYU WIRELESS at 6.75 GHz, 16.95 GHz, 28 GHz, 73 GHz, and 142 GHz, using a 1 GHz wideband time-domain based sliding correlation channel sounder in the indoor hotspot (InH) scenario in both line-of-sight (LOS) and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) channel conditions. Specifically, the derived CI, FI, and ABG path loss model parameters for 7–24 GHz and 0.5–100 GHz frequency ranges in this article were submitted in Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) to validate Technical Report (TR) 38.901 InH path loss models, as part of the release (Rel) 19 study on "Channel Model Validation of TR 38.901 for 7–24 GHz." Furthermore, the results in this paper provide critical insights into understanding large-scale path loss, comparing different path loss models, and extending the path loss models standardized by 3GPP and ITU for the InH scenario, which is essential for advancing next-generation wireless systems.

title: KARMA: Knowledge-Aware Resource Management and Autoscaling for Edge Workloads
authors: Dimitrios Selis, Kostas Ramantas, Luis Alonso, John S Vardakas, Christos Verikoukis
abstract: In the realm of modern telecommunication ecosystems, resource-aware management is critical in ensuring quality of service (QoS) while enabling proactive, zero-touch operations. Furthermore, recent advances in software defined networking (SDN) have significantly contributed to beyond 5G (B5G) network optimization, offering innovative solutions to tackle their growing complexity. Moreover, the inherently non-convex nature of service provisioning challenges presents critical obstacles to delivering a seamless user experience. This paper introduces a novel framework that integrates Dueling Double Deep Q-Networks (Dueling DDQN) within a cloud-native edge infrastructure, enabling zero-touch service scaling. By harnessing the advanced capabilities of deep reinforcement learning (DRL), the proposed method autonomously identifies and addresses scaling demands, thereby improving overall network capabilities. The effectiveness of our approach is validated through experiments conducted on a multi-node Kubernetes testbed, demonstrating its ability to alleviate performance bottlenecks in distributed environments.

title: Stability-Oriented Design of Adaptive Model Predictive Control with LCL-Filtered for Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Nottakorn Sukmanont, Kanet Katchasuwanmanee, Fathin Saifur Rahman, Thongchart Kerdphol
abstract: In a future grid transitioning from conventional synchronous generation to clean-energy-dominated operation, grid-forming inverters (GFMIs) are pivotal for the seamless integration of renewable sources. However, conventional proportional–integral (PI) control has limited robustness to operating-point shifts, disturbances, and cyber threats. This study proposes an adaptive model predictive control (AMPC) scheme for GFMIs that combines constraint-aware optimal control with online adaptation and incorporates a Kalman-filter-based state estimator to mitigate measurement noise and reduce vulnerability to sensor spoofing and other cyber-induced disturbances. The AMPC scheme is evaluated in simulation under nominal conditions, in adaptation-critical scenarios including cyber-attack emulation, and during fault events that drive transitions to islanded operation. Results show that AMPC achieves superior transient and steady-state performance, with improved regulation of voltage, current, and frequency compared with PI control and non-adaptive MPC, and it demonstrates enhanced transient harmonic performance. These outcomes demonstrate the controller’s robustness across diverse operating scenarios and highlight its potential to support reliable renewable-rich power systems, aligning with long-term pathways toward carbon neutrality.

title: Benchmarking AI Inference Efficiency in Public and Private Clouds: an MLPerf-Based Comparative Study
authors: Carl Beitsayadeh, Pamayla E. Darbyshire
abstract: AI inference is emerging as a major driver of energy consumption in large-scale computing environments, yet most existing research focuses on the training phase of machine learning models. This cross-sectional secondary analysis benchmarks inference throughput and energy efficiency across public and private cloud infrastructures using 739 standardized submissions from MLPerf Inference v5.0. Efficiency was estimated as throughput normalized by vendor-reported thermal design power (TDP), providing a reproducible proxy for performance-per-watt comparison. Grounded in systems theory, the analysis treats inference efficiency as an emergent property of interacting hardware, infrastructure, and workload subsystems. Statistical methods included Welch's t-tests, one- and two-way ANOVA, and linear and log-linear regression with residual diagnostics and model-fit evaluation. Results indicated no significant cloud-type effect, significant differences across accelerator models (with NVIDIA H200-SXM-141GB exhibiting approximately 45% higher median efficiency), and no cloud–accelerator interaction. The log-linear specification provided the best model fit, suggesting multiplicative scaling in efficiency across accelerator generations. The study introduces a transparent benchmarking framework for estimating inference efficiency using open MLPerf data and highlights inference optimization as an important pathway toward sustainable AI infrastructure.

title: Advances in Reliability and Artificial Intelligence for Power Electronic Systems
authors: Frede Blaabjerg, Kaichen Zhang, Yubo Song, Yichi Zhang, Bo Yao, Mateja Novak, Shuai Zhao, Subham Sahoo, Ariya Sangwongwanich, Huai Wang
abstract: Power Electronics is a key technology for the global electrification including sustainable energy production, enabling large energy savings and electrification of the transport sector. In this context, reliability is key to achieve lower cost, lower material consumption and high confidence of a product – and recently artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a key discipline to further shape the next generation of power electronics technology. At the same time the power electronic systems are growing in complexity including both hardware and software, thereby reliability considerations in design, testing, and operation are important. Further, AI offers new opportunities for condition monitoring, better lifetime prediction, and more robust decision-making. This paper provides a roadmap for advancing reliability engineering and AI applications in power electronic systems by reviewing recent advances and trends covering system-level reliability practices, multiple related standards, critical components (both active and passive), mission-profile-based analysis and testing approaches, as well as uncertainty-aware reliability analysis. It further discusses how AI-based methods can enhance reliability prediction by doing predictive maintenance and optimized design. Finally, the paper outlines future challenges and opportunities, like how to deal with new power devices including their packaging, hybrid testing concepts, applying virtual sensors and digital twins using AI, analyzing complicated systems and also how to integrate circular economy into the design of power electronics technology.

title: 2025 Index IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques Vol. 73
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques

title: Global Error Analysis and Improvement for Magnetic Target Inversion Algorithms
authors: Shengxin Lin, Yi Li, Leyang Sun, Yinxi Jin, Donghua Pan, Liyi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Magnetic target inversion technology has significant applications in biomedicine, unexploded ordnance detection, and vehicle tracking. However, traditional inversion algorithms suffer from inaccurate areas, limiting the inversion accuracy. This article establishes a global error model that simultaneously accounts for the effects of the magnetic target’s position and posture, revealing the distribution of inaccurate areas. Error analysis demonstrates that the inaccurate areas differ across various inversion algorithms. By switching between these inversion algorithms, we propose an improved inversion algorithm. Simulation results show that the improved inversion algorithm reduces the size of the inaccurate area to 0.20%. Experimental results in a magnetic shielding room (MSR) indicate that the new inversion algorithm improves positioning accuracy by more than 59%. This improvement allows magnetic target inversion technology to determine the position and posture of the magnetic target with greater accuracy, significantly boosting its effectiveness and reliability across various applications.

title: Joint Probing and Scheduling for Cache-Aided Hybrid Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Zhou Zhang, Yizhu Wang, Saman Atapattu, Sumei Sun
abstract: Caching is crucial in hybrid satellite-terrestrial networks to reduce latency, optimize throughput, and improve data availability by storing frequently accessed content closer to users, especially in bandwidth-limited satellite systems, requiring strategic Medium Access Control (MAC) layer. This paper addresses throughput optimization in satellite-terrestrial integrated networks through opportunistic cooperative caching. We propose a joint probing and scheduling strategy to enhance content retrieval efficiency. The strategy leverages the LEO satellite to probe satellite-to-ground links and cache states of multiple cooperative terrestrial stations, enabling dynamic user scheduling for content delivery. Using an optimal stopping theoretic approach with two levels of incomplete information, we make real-time decisions on satellite-terrestrial hybrid links and caching probing. Our threshold-based strategy optimizes probing and scheduling, significantly improving average system throughput by exploiting cooperative caching, satellite-terrestrial link transmission, and time diversity from dynamic user requests. Simulation results validate the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed strategies.

title: GLOBECOM 2025 TOC
authors: N/A

title: A Novel Unsupervised Separation of Surface Discharge Signals Employing Empirical ModeDecomposition and Second-Order Difference Plot
authors: Piklu Das, Soumya Chatterjee, Chiranjib Koley
abstract: Surface discharge is a common type of partial discharge (PD) that puts any high-voltage (HV) system at significant risk. Accurate identification and segregation of various surface discharges is important to prevent the premature failure of HV system. To address this issue, this study proposes a data-driven clustering framework utilizing signal decomposition technique followed by nonlinear feature extraction inspired by chaos theory. To this end, four types of surface discharge defects have been emulated and for each discharge, time domain PD signals were recorded using a high-frequency current transformer (HFCT) sensor designed to operate from 300kHz–10MHz. The PD signals captured by the HFCT sensor were initially decomposed using empirical mode decomposition (EMD) to derive intrinsic mode functions (IMFs), which capture inherent oscillatory modes of the PD signals. Following this,second-order difference plot (SODP) of the extracted IMFs were generated to analyze the dynamic and chaotic behavior of PD signals. From each SODP plot, several features have been extracted and following feature selection using analysis of variance (ANOVA), top three features were used as inputs to a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-means clustering algorithm for separation of different surface discharge signals into distinct clusters. In addition, experiments have been conducted on real-life insulator defects to verify the practicability of the proposed method. It has been observed that the proposed method employing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-means clustering and SODP plots can segregate between different surface discharge PD sources accurately. The proposed PD detection method offers a reliable solution for unsupervised PD signal categorization in HV applications.

title: Digital Twin-Guided Energy Management over Real-Time Pub/Sub Protocol in 6G Smart Cities
authors: Kubra Duran, Lal Verda Cakir, Sana Ullah Jan, Kerem Gursu, Berk Canberk
abstract: Although the emergence of 6G IoT networks has accelerated the deployment of enhanced smart city services, the resource limitations of IoT devices remain as a significant problem. Given this limitation, meeting the low-latency service requirement of 6G networks becomes even more challenging. However, existing 6G IoT management strategies lack real-time operation and mostly rely on discrete actions, which are insufficient to optimise energy consumption. To address these, in this study, we propose a Digital Twin (DT)-guided energy management framework to jointly handle the low latency and energy efficiency challenges in 6G IoT networks. In this framework, we provide the twin models through a distributed overlay network and handle the dynamic updates between the data layer and the upper layers of the DT over the Real-Time Publish Subscribe (RTPS) protocol. We also design a Reinforcement Learning (RL) engine with a novel formulated reward function to provide optimal data update times for each of the IoT devices. The RL engine receives a diverse set of environment states from the What-if engine and runs Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) to output continuous actions to the IoT devices. Based on our simulation results, we observe that the proposed framework achieves a 37% improvement in 95th percentile latency and a 30% reduction in energy consumption compared to the existing literature.

title: Hierarchical Transfer Learning: A Key to Enabling CSI Feedback for 6G Extreme Massive MIMO
authors: Jungsuk Baik, Byeonghun Hwang, Bongsung Seo, Min Jang, Juho Lee, Charlie Jianzhong Zhang
abstract: With the advent of extreme massive multiple-input multiple-output (X-MIMO) systems and the emerging Frequency Range 3 (FR3, 7.125–24.25 GHz) for 6G networks, the dimensionality of channel state information (CSI) has increased significantly. However, to ensure scalable operation and avoid an excessive increase in overhead, the feedback size is expected to increase only marginally. This constraint necessitates an extremely high compression ratio during CSI feedback, which in turn severely degrades the reconstruction performance of standardized codebooks. Moreover, even when employing AI methods, training models from scratch under such severe compression conditions leads to vanishing or exploding gradients, resulting in unstable training and diminished performance. In this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical transfer learning with progressive compression (HTL-PC) method to address these challenges. Our approach leverages a pretrained autoencoder extraction block from a large-feedback model and progressively transfers it to models with smaller latent spaces, thereby enabling stable training under severe compression conditions. Extensive system-level simulations demonstrate that the proposed HTL-PC method achieves remarkable improvements in squared generalized cosine similarity and downlink cell throughput over benchmark techniques.

title: Handover Algorithm for Achieving Ubiquitous Connectivity in D2C Satellite Systems
authors: Warangrat Wiriya, Ryusuke Utsunomiya, Mokoto Ono, Hyungmin Ha, Ryoji Osaka
abstract: This paper presents a novel handover algorithm designed to address the challenges of ubiquitous connectivity in LEO-based Direct-to-Cell (D2C) satellite systems, specifically focusing on maximizing both coverage and link quality across the service area. Unlike existing approaches, our method jointly optimizes the service and feeder links for a more realistic solution. We propose a new bipartite graph structure, representing satellites and satellite ground cells aligned with earth-fixed beams, and utilize two bipartite graphs to enable simultaneous optimization of both links. The algorithm maximizes both coverage and link quality in the service link while determining optimal gateway locations and antenna numbers in the feeder link to support the maximization targets in service link. Simulations based on a Walker-Delta satellite constellation and real map-based scenarios demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms random gateway placement, improving coverage by up to 4% and increasing the average SNR by approximately up to 3 dB across all cells.

title: Hybrid-Space Interaction Network for Single-Image Super-Resolution in Industrial Intelligent Detection
authors: Haoyuan Li, Ruiyun Yu, Bingyang Guo, Shi Zhen, Zhengtao Zhang
abstract: With the continuous advancement of modern industrial intelligent manufacturing, rigorous defect detection has become indispensable, leading to a growing demand for more precise detection methods. Super-resolution technology, recognized for its potential to enhance image details and support comprehensive inspections, has emerged as a pivotal method in many industrial scenarios. Nevertheless, current super-resolution techniques continue to face significant limitations. In particular, single-image super-resolution encounters challenges in preserving interregional correlations and preventing information loss during reconstruction. To address these challenges, this article proposes a hybrid-space interaction network (HsINet), incorporating a hybrid-level multichannel interaction (HMI) module with multilevel self-integration attention (MSIA) and a double-way supervision module (DwS) with self-integration attention (SIA). The HMI leverages a hybrid-level approach to extract richer features through DwS modules at various levels while preserving detailed feature-space information. MSIA in HMI enhances the correlation of information between different levels and channels. DwS utilizes SIA to strengthen relationships between image regions and employs mutual supervision to prevent high-frequency information degradation. In addition, we collect industrial datasets from various scenarios and propose a super-resolution dataset for ceramic packaging substrates in the chip manufacturing field. Experiments on multiple industrial datasets and our automated optical inspection equipment reveal that HsINet exhibits exceptional super-resolution performance in both quantitative and qualitative analyzes and excels in downstream defect detection tasks.

title: Satellite-GS: Enhanced 2D Gaussian Splatting for Robust Satellite Reconstruction
authors: Jingyuan Wang, Minghao Liu, Xi Yang, Xin Wei, Lu Wang, Nannan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate 3D reconstruction of satellites from satellite-to-satellite (STS) optical imagery is crucial for Space Situational Awareness (SSA), yet it is significantly challenged by the extreme space environment. Conventional 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) pipelines degrade when facing STS-specific challenges, particularly in surface reconstruction quality. To overcome these obstacles, we propose Satellite-GS, a robust and precise STS reconstruction framework built upon enhanced 2D Gaussian Splatting (2DGS), which enables high-quality surface reconstruction with fewer parameters. Initially, we introduce a Satellite Image Deblurring (SID) model to perform motion deblurring and restore texture details. Subsequently, the 2DGS Enhancing introduces key innovations: KNN-based floater removal eliminates outliers and a Pixel-Enhance (PE) loss that preserves unlit satellite structures from erosion under extreme radiance imbalance.
