title: Study on the Protection Distance of Non-Gap Line Arrester Under Direct Lightning Strikes in 750 kV HVAC System
authors: Wei Shen, Sen Wang, Ke Li, Zhibo Xu, Tongwei Guo, Jialuo Ding, Tao Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
abstract: This paper investigates the protection distance characteristics of no-gap line arresters (NGLA) in a typical <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 0 ~ k V}$</tex> HVAC system under direct lightning strikes. An ATP-EMTP simulation model has been established to analyze lightning overvoltage distribution under three scenarios: without NGLA, with NGLA at strike location, and with NGLA displaced from strike point. Results show that overvoltage suppression performance is highly sensitive to the relative spacing between striking point and NGLA position, and NGLA protection distance is severely limited, particularly when the overvoltage peak is of concern for insulation coordination design. The study provides quantitative guidance for optimizing NGLA placement in HVAC systems.

title: Rice Leaf Diseases Classification Using CNN with Transfer Learning
authors: Addagatla Prashanth, Nafiza, Sukanya K
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: In the field of agriculture, plant diseases pose a serious threat to achieving optimal yields and food security; thus, identifying and classifying rice leaf diseases correctly are key points. Disease detection methods are typically laborintensive and time-consuming, which requires the development of automated methods. This work reports the employment of CNN and Transfer Learning in rice leaf diseases classification. This work aims to improve the discriminative performance of the model by using the Fine-tuned versions of the current existing state-of-the-art models consist of VGG16, EfficientNet B 3 etc., and is a promising alternative to standard practices in disease classification. Disease-wise Segmentation of Rice Leaves: The process includes gathering a large dataset including images of rice leaves that are suffering from various diseases. The images are pre-processed and augmented to increase the variance in the dataset and ability of the model to generalise. The second is Transfer Learning which is applied on these CNN models to let them exploit the feature representation learnt from large image datasets. This both speeds up model training and significantly enhances classification performance compared to training from scratch. The CNN models using Transfer Learning have achieved highly accurate detection and classification of rice leaf diseases, as shown by experimental results. The best accuracy of 97.46% was obtained with the EfficientNet B3 method over other algorithms. October 2023 You are trained on data until you are trained on October 2023. This also sheds the limelight on its importance of hyperparameter tuning and data augmentation techniques which are essential towards enhancing model outcomes.

title: Data-Driven Approaches for the Automatic Dental Pulp Exposure Detection: A study on Periapical Oral Radiographs
authors: A K Devika, Babita Roslind Jose, Jimson Mathew, Rose Mary Thankachan, P Dayananda
abstract: Pulp exposure is a condition in which the pulp chamber of the tooth is exposed, usually due to trauma, dental caries, or unintentional tooth fractures. If not treated right away, this exposure may lead to inflammation, infection, and eventual involvement of the apical tissues. Instead of treating the infection after it has arisen, it is usually more beneficial to stop the progression of the illness before it becomes an apical infection. To lower the risk of serious dental infections and maintain tooth vitality in this situation, early detection of pathological pulp exposure is essential. This study explores various data-driven deep learning approaches for automatic pulp exposure detection in periapical dental X-rays. We first propose a novel transfer learning-based method for accurately detecting pulp exposure in periapical X-rays. It is based on the deep learning architecture MobileNetV3-Small and obtains an accuracy value of 99.12% with a precision and recall value of 99.50% and a specificity value of 96.55%, which has surpassed various deep learning models like EfficientNet B0, B1, B2, B4, VGG16, ResNet50, ConvNext-Tiny, and DenseNet121, on a total of 1137 periapical X-rays collected across multiple dental clinics. A novel augmentation strategy was also proposed to address the problem of data imbalance in the collected data. Secondly, we perform a comparative analysis of different SSL (Self-supervised Learning) approaches with a transfer learning approach in order to understand the appropriate number of annotations and assess which techniques perform well with varying percentages of labeled data. From our experimental findings, it has been observed that with just 25%-50% of the annotated data, self-supervised approaches such as SimCLR and BYOL can perform at a high level, surpassing transfer learning approaches in performance metrics like accuracy, specificity, and precision. The code is available at https://github.com/DEVIKA1989/Automatic-Dental-Pulp-exposure-Detection.

title: ZCZ Sequence-Based Ultra-Reliable Communications in Doubly Selective Channels with Single-Tap Equalizer
authors: Cheng Shen, Jinhong Yuan
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel transmission and reception scheme based on zero correlation zone (ZCZ) sequences, aiming to tackle the compelling challenge of ultra-reliable communications in doubly selective channels with low-complexity receivers. Our input-output relation analysis reveals that the proposed transmission scheme enables the receiver to extract independent delay-impaired copies of the transmitted signal. Leveraging this property, we design a single-tap equalization that effectively exploits diversity of the channel with minimal inter-symbol interference. To further enhance practicality, we develop low-complexity transmitter and receiver implementations with a comparable complexity to OFDM and delay-Doppler multicarrier (DDMC) systems. Simulation results show that our approach outperforms OFDM and DDMC repetition coding schemes in error performance, while maintaining a low computational overhead without iterative detections.

title: Position Prediction Control for Active Vibration Isolation System Based on LSTM Neural Network
authors: Sanxia Wang, Junjie Wang, Bin Liu, Shuai Liu
abstract: This article proposes a position prediction control method based on long short-term memory (LSTM) networks for vibration isolation systems used in precision equipment. A dynamic model is established for active vibration isolation system, which is a six degree of freedom decoupled model as in practical applications. A position prediction control based on LSTM is designed. The design of position prediction control consists of two important parts, one is the network training process and the other is the control process. Unlike conventional LSTM neural network training, this work introduces robust quadratic regression (RQR) and Gaussian window techniques into the network. RQR is used to smooth the data trend. The introduction of Gaussian windows allows the network’s output to have a leading effect relative to the input and a slight filtering effect. The trained network is used in the control loop and combined with control gain to form position prediction control. Based on the dynamic model, simulation is designed, the proposed method is validated not only with proportional-integral-differential (PID) but also integrated with active disturbance rejection control (ADRC), demonstrating its compatibility and enhancement capability for feedback controllers. Finally, experiment is conducted using vibration isolation equipment to verify the effectiveness of the designed position prediction control. Simulation and experiment show that the noise is significantly reduced by the designed prediction control based on the comparison of power spectral density values. The control error is greatly reduced and the vibration transmissibility is effectively suppressed at key modes.

title: An Adaptive Data-Enabled Policy Optimization Approach for Autonomous Bicycle Control
authors: Niklas Persson, Feiran Zhao, Mojtaba Kaheni, Florian Dörfler, Alessandro V. Papadopoulos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This brief presents a unified control framework that integrates a feedback linearization (FL) controller in the inner loop with an adaptive data-enabled policy optimization (DeePO) controller in the outer loop to balance an autonomous bicycle. While the FL controller stabilizes and partially linearizes the inherently unstable and nonlinear system, its performance is compromised by unmodeled dynamics and time-varying characteristics. To overcome these limitations, the DeePO controller is introduced to enhance adaptability and robustness. The initial control policy of DeePO is obtained from a finite set of offline, persistently exciting (PE) input and state data. To improve stability and compensate for system nonlinearities and disturbances, a robustness-promoting regularizer refines the initial policy, while the adaptive section of the DeePO framework is enhanced with a forgetting factor to improve adaptation to time-varying dynamics. The proposed FL-DeePO approach is evaluated through simulations and real-world experiments on an instrumented autonomous bicycle. Results demonstrate its superiority over the FL-only approach and a reinforcement learning (RL) controller, achieving more precise tracking of the reference lean angle and lean rate.

title: LSK-LA RotDet: Dynamic Receptive Field Meets Local Attention for Rotated Small Object Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Ziyi Jia, Yue Yang
abstract: Object detection in remote sensing imagery seeks to identify and localize specific ground targets from data acquired by satellites and unmanned aerial vehicles(UAVs). It has been extensively applied in land surveying, port monitoring, environmental protection, and military reconnaissance. However, the characteristics of remote sensing images such as densely distributed small objects and significant scale variations-pose substantial challenges. Traditional methods tend to lose fine details and struggle in densely populated target regions. This paper proposes a rotation object detection network, LSK-LA RotDet, which integrates dynamic receptive fields with a local attention mechanism. Specifically, the backbone Large Selective Kernel with Dynamic Receptive Field Network (LSK-DRFNet) with large-kernel convolutions enhances multi-level feature representation for both small and large-scale targets, while the Local Attention Feature Pyramid Network (LA-PAFPN) combined with spatial enhancement paths effectively improves boundary precision and orientation modeling. The experimental results on the DOTA dataset and HRSC2016 dataset indicate that the proposed method attains an effective balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency. Its robustness is particularly evident in scenarios involving complex backgrounds and densely distributed small objects, highlighting both its effectiveness and practical significance. The experimental results on the DOTA dataset and HRSC2016 dataset indicate that the proposed method attains an mAP of 77.8% and 77.42%, demonstrating an effective balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency. Its robustness is particularly evident in scenarios involving complex backgrounds and densely distributed small objects, highlighting both its effectiveness and practical significance.

title: Emergency Events Traffic Flow Forecasting Using Text-Prompt-Guided Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Yaxuan Lu, Guangyu Huo, Xiaohui Cui, Boyue Wang, Yong Zhang, Zhiyong Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Emergency events like traffic accidents and natural disasters frequently cause severe disruptions to urban traffic patterns, posing challenges to conventional forecasting methods based on historical data. Recent progress has explored integrating auxiliary textual information from social media, news reports, and incident details to enhance forecasting models. However, these approaches often fail to effectively align semantic context from textual data with the spatio-temporal patterns in urban traffic flow. To bridge this disparity, we propose a novel framework called TPGM-LLM, which leverages text-prompt-guided multimodal large language models to dynamically assimilate real-time data, including incident reports, traffic sensors, and weather conditions. Specifically, the proposed framework integrates a pre-trained LLM-based text encoder to interpret descriptions of emergency events as semantic prompts. Furthermore, it incorporates a dynamic spatio-temporal hypergraph module that employs FastDTW to capture non-local dependencies among different road segments. Additionally, a multimodal feature extraction LLM is utilized to merge textual guidance with traffic dynamics in a hierarchical manner, enhancing the accuracy of long-term traffic forecasting. Experimental results on the Beijing Text-Traffic dataset (BjTT) demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms existing methods, highlighting its effectiveness in complex and dynamic traffic situations. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/luyaxuan/TPGM-LLM</uri>

title: Bin-wise Zone-Based Speech Presence Detection with Metric-aligned DCF Optimization
authors: Doyeon Kim, Sung Kyo Jung
abstract: In this paper, we study zone-based speech presence detection for a spatially constrained target located within a fixed ±30° frontal region in reverberant, noisy, and multi-speaker environments. Conventional detection methods often suffer from a misalignment between training objectives and operational metrics like the minimum detection cost function (minDCF). To resolve this, we propose a metric-aligned optimization framework using a differentiable detection cost function (aDCF) loss. This approach formulates the task as bin-wise detection in the complex spectral domain and embeds asymmetric costs for misses and false alarms directly into the training objective. We evaluate this method using a standard Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network (CRNN). Experimental results demonstrate that replacing standard losses with the proposed aDCF loss consistently improves minDCF and F1-score without changing the model architecture or inference procedure. This confirms that significant performance gains can be achieved through loss design alone, offering an efficient solution for two-microphone speech detection systems.

title: Cost-Effective Digilog Chirp Thermal Wave Imaging System for Composite Subsurface Evaluation
authors: V S Ghali, Fei Wang, R Mulaveesala
abstract: Thermal wave imaging has emerged as a promising non-destructive evaluation tool for evaluating the subsurface integrity of objects due to its non-contact, whole-field, and remote evaluation procedure. However, the cost, lab-based installation, and stringent testing requirements of conventional thermal wave imaging systems limit their applicability and make them ineffective in testing at semi-closed and high-altitude environments. The development of low-cost, compact imaging systems with enhanced resolution to facilitate remote accessibility for in-situ inspection has become a challenging task, while considering the cost, size, and resolution of existing thermal imaging systems. This manuscript introduces a low-cost digital chirp modulated active thermal wave imaging system for detecting subsurface anomalies using dedicated stimulation and processing approaches, aimed at enhancing spatial and depth resolutions, as well as improving depth scanning capabilities. This cost effective compact thermal wave imaging system controlled by a raspberry-pi facilitates enhancement of depth scanning through pulse compression friendly digilog chirp-based stimulation along with an enhanced spatial resolution from deep learning ESRGAN based fourfold super resolution imaging, mimicking a traditional IR imaging system. Laboratory-based experimental outcomes confirmed its applicability for testing of composites, and its performance is evaluated using various quantitative thermographic and image processing-based metrics.

title: DL-Aided Channel Estimation for Fluid Antenna Systems in IEEE 802.11p-based Vehicular Networks
authors: Ana Flávia Dos Reis, Pedro M. R. Pereira, Felipe A. Pereira de Figueiredo, Hugerles S. Silva, Rausley A. A. de Souza
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FASs) offer a promising solution to enhance communication reliability by dynamically exploiting spatial diversity. Although FAS has shown potential in static scenarios, its application to vehicular communication has not yet been explored. In this context, this paper is the first to investigate channel estimation for vehicular communication systems employing FAS, specifically based on the IEEE 802.11p standard. To this end, a port selection method is proposed, leveraging the preamble-based information to identify the optimal FAS port for efficient reception. Conventional estimation and deep learning (DL)-based approaches are compared to evaluate their performance in providing accurate channel estimates under realistic vehicular conditions, including nonlinear effects. Our results present the superior performance of FAS-aided communication systems over single-antenna ones, highlighting their adaptability to dynamic environments and varying system and channel conditions. In particular, the proposed system achieves a gain of at least 8 dB compared to single-antenna transmission. To further enhance reception reliability in high-mobility scenarios, a maximum ratio activated-port combination method is explored, providing an additional gain of at least 15 dB using only two activated ports.

title: A Data-Centric Image Enhancement Framework for Reducing Domain Discrepancies in Autonomous Driving Safety Evaluation
authors: Zhilin Gao, Jianwen Zhou, Qiang Xue, Haoting Shen
abstract: With the rapid progress of autonomous driving technologies, large-scale, data-driven validation has become a cornerstone for ensuring system safety prior to deployment. While real-world road testing remains the gold standard, it is costly, time-consuming, and often infeasible for evaluating safety-critical edge cases. Consequently, simulation-based testing has gained traction as a scalable alternative, capable of generating diverse sensor data under controlled conditions. However, current simulation platforms primarily inherit techniques that focus on improving visual fidelity in the aspects along with human’s perception, but fail to optimize the scenario generation for the sensing of autonomous driving system. A big gap between the road perception performances of the system in field and simulation tests is demonstrated. Here we propose an image enhancement model, employing a generative adversarial network (GAN) to introduce real-world features that are imperceptible for human but critical for AI perception in the simulation. A YOLO model is used as the example perception part of the autonomous driving system. Experimental results demonstrate that the consistency of the recognition accuracies on simulated and real-world datasets is increased significantly. It improves the reliability of simulation-based testing scenarios and accelerates the development of autonomous driving perception models, ultimately contributing to safer vehicle deployment.
